## שֶׁ (id: 007126000000000)

שֶׁ
===

שֶׁ (H7945) — שַׁ

conj · אֲשֶׁר

Description

that, which, where, when, how \= a linker introducing a proposition that gives additional information regarding an explicit or implicit object, location, time, or manner

[GEN 6:3](https://ref.ly/Gen6:3) · [JDG 5:7](https://ref.ly/Judg5:7) · [JDG 5:7](https://ref.ly/Judg5:7) · [JDG 6:17](https://ref.ly/Judg6:17) · [JDG 7:12](https://ref.ly/Judg7:12) · [JDG ## שׁאב (id: 007127000000000)

שׁאב
====

שׁאב (H7579)

verb · מַשְׁאָב

Move \| verb qal

to draw (water) \= causative action by which humans let down a bucket\-like container attached to a rope into a well in order to draw water from it

## שׁאג (id: 007128000000000)

שׁאג
====

שׁאג (H7580)

verb · שְׁאָגָה

Sounds \| verb qal \| ≈ נער

to roar, to groan \= action by which animals, humans, or deities produce a full, deep, prolonged sound as made by a lion; ≈ associated with aggression, but also with suffering

שְׁאָגָה
========

שְׁאָגָה (H7581)

noun f · שׁאג

Sounds

roar, groaning \= action by which animals, humans, or deities produce a full, deep, prolonged sound as made by a lion

## שׁאה (id: 007130000000000)

שׁאה
====

שׁאה (H7582 · H7583)

verb · מְשֹׁואָה · שְׁאֹול · שָׁאֹון · שְׁאִיָּה · שֵׁאת · שֹׁוא · שֹׁואָה · תְּשֻׁאָה

Impact \| verb qal

to lie waste, to lie in ruins \= state in which buildings and infrastructure have been destroyed

[ISA 6:11](https://ref.ly/Isa6:11) · [ISA 6:11](https://ref.ly/Isa6:11)

Impact \| verb hif

to lay waste \= causative action by which humans bring destruction to buildings and infrastructure

[2KI 19:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:25) · [ISA 37:26](https://ref.ly/Isa37:26)

verb

Unsafe \| verb nif \| ≈ המה · הָמֹון · שָׁאֹון

to roar \= process by which a political entity, like a nation or kingdom, is in a state of unrest, often meaning that they are contemplating rebellion or planning to wage war; ◄ described in terms of the sound produced by turbulent waters

[ISA 17:12](https://ref.ly/Isa17:12) · [ISA 17:13](https://ref.ly/Isa17:13)

verb

See \| verb hit

to gaze (at something) \= action by which humans fix their eyes on an object for a prolonged period of time

[GEN 24:21](https://ref.ly/Gen24:21)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 6:11; 2 Kings 19:25; Isaiah 37:26; Isaiah 17:12; Isaiah 17:13; Genesis 24:21


## שְׁאֹול (id: 007131000000000)

שְׁאֹול
=======

שְׁאֹול (H7585)

noun c · שׁאה

Regions Under the Earth \| ≈ אֲבַדֹּון · בֹּור · חֹשֶׁךְ · מָוֶת · שַׁחַת \| ≠ חַיִּים · שָׁמַיִם

world of the dead \= abode of the dead, a place of darkness and silence, located deep in the earth and inaccessible, where there is neither compassion nor wisdom; ≈ regarded as a place that is never satisfied; often personified; going there before one's time is considered a punishment

## שָׁאֹון (id: 007134000000000)

שָׁאֹון
=======

שָׁאֹון (H7588)

noun m · שׁאה

Sounds \| ≈ הָמֹון

roaring, seething \= process by which turbulent waters produce a loud and intimidating noise

[PSA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ps40:3) · [PSA 65:8](https://ref.ly/Ps65:8) · [PSA 65:8](https://ref.ly/Ps65:8) · [ISA 17:12](https://ref.ly/Isa17:12) · [ISA 17:13](https://ref.ly/Isa17:13)

Speak \| ≈ מָשֹׂושׂ · קֹול · תְּרוּעָה

noise, tumult \= process by which humans produce loud sounds, either as individuals or groups, in order to give expression to aggression, revelry, or excessive pride

[PSA 74:23](https://ref.ly/Ps74:23) · [ISA 24:8](https://ref.ly/Isa24:8) · [ISA 66:6](https://ref.ly/Isa66:6) · [JER 46:17](https://ref.ly/Jer46:17) · [JER 51:55](https://ref.ly/Jer51:55) · [AMO 2:2](https://ref.ly/Amos2:2)

Groups \| ≈ הָמֹון

noisy crowd, tumultuous throng literally: noise, tumult; hence: \= a group of people in the process of making loud noise

[ISA 5:14](https://ref.ly/Isa5:14)

Unsafe \| ≈ הָמֹון

uproar, roaring, unrest literally: roaring; hence: \= process by which a political entity, like a nation or kingdom, is in a state of unrest, often meaning that they are contemplating rebellion or planning to wage war; ◄ described in terms of the sound produced by turbulent waters

[ISA 13:4](https://ref.ly/Isa13:4) · [ISA 17:12](https://ref.ly/Isa17:12) · [ISA 25:5](https://ref.ly/Isa25:5) · [JER 25:31](https://ref.ly/Jer25:31) · [JER 48:45](https://ref.ly/Jer48:45) · [HOS 10:14](https://ref.ly/Hos10:14)

Reinier de Blois

Speak \| verb qal

## שְׁאֵלָה (id: 007139000000000)

========

שְׁאֵלָה (A7595 · H7596) — שְׁאֵלָא, שְׁאָלָה

noun f · שׁאל

Wish \| Speak \| ≈ בַּקָּשָׁה

request \= action by which humans experience a desire for something to happen and communicate this to (other) humans or deities in the hope that they will intenvene on their behalf

Think \| ≈ פִּתְגָם

decision, verdict \= a decision by someone in authority on a certain issue

## שׁאף (id: 007144000000000)

שׁאף
====

שׁאף (H7602)

verb

Blow \| verb qal \| ≈ נשׁם

to gasp, to pant \= process by which humans or animals strain for breath with an open mouth, ◄ caused by exhaustion or pain

[ECC 1:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:5) · [ISA 42:14](https://ref.ly/Isa42:14)

Blow \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁאף רוּחַ

to sniff the air \= action by which animals draw up air through the nose in an audible way; ◄ out of hunger or a desire to mate; ► in order to detect the smell of food or of a mate; ≈ associated with longing and desire

[JER 2:24](https://ref.ly/Jer2:24) · [JER 14:6](https://ref.ly/Jer14:6)

Wish \| verb qal \| ≈ קוה

to pant after, to long for literally: to pant after something; hence: \= state in which humans wish strongly for something to happen

[JOB 5:5](https://ref.ly/Job5:5) · [JOB 7:2](https://ref.ly/Job7:2) · [JOB 36:20](https://ref.ly/Job36:20) · [PSA 119:131](https://ref.ly/Ps119:131)

verb

Impact \| verb qal

to press \= causative action by which humans violently press an object against or into another object

[AMO 2:7](https://ref.ly/Amos2:7)

Unsafe \| verb qal \| ≈ לחם · לחץ · שׁבת · שׁמם

to oppress \= pattern of actions by which humans subject individuals or entire communities to cruelty and/or injustice

[PSA 56:2](https://ref.ly/Ps56:2) · [PSA 56:3](https://ref.ly/Ps56:3) · [PSA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Ps57:4) · [EZK 36:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:3) · [AMO 8:4](https://ref.ly/Amos8:4)

## שְׁאֵר (id: 007146000000000)

שְׁאֵר
======

שְׁאֵר (H7607)

noun m · שׁאר

\| ≈ בָּשָׂר

flesh, meat poetic term: \= the soft substance in the body of humans and animals that consists of muscle tissue and fat

[PSA 78:20](https://ref.ly/Ps78:20) · [PSA 78:27](https://ref.ly/Ps78:27) · [PRO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Prov5:11) · [MIC 3:2](https://ref.ly/Mic3:2) · [MIC 3:3](https://ref.ly/Mic3:3)

\| ≈ בָּשָׂר · שְׁאָר \| ≠ לֵבָב

body poetic term: \= the physical body of humans and animals, not including the mind and the inner person

[PSA 73:26](https://ref.ly/Ps73:26)

Food

food literally: flesh; hence: \= food provided to someone to live on

[EXO 21:10](https://ref.ly/Exod21:10)

Relatives \| ≈ בָּשָׂר

relative literally: (one's) flesh; hence: \= a person connected by blood or marriage

[LEV 18:6](https://ref.ly/Lev18:6) · [LEV 18:12](https://ref.ly/Lev18:12) · [LEV 18:13](https://ref.ly/Lev18:13) · [LEV 20:19](https://ref.ly/Lev20:19) · [LEV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Lev21:2) · [LEV 25:49](https://ref.ly/Lev25:49) · [NUM 27:11](https://ref.ly/Num27:11) · [JER 51:35](https://ref.ly/Jer51:35)

Self

self literally: (one's) flesh; hence: \= a reference to one's self

[PRO 11:17](https://ref.ly/Prov11:17)

שְׁאָר
======

rest, remnant \= a limited number of a larger quantity of objects remaining in a place after part of it was dealt with another way

Groups \| ≈ פְּלֵיטָה

\| ≈ בָּשָׂר · שְׁאֵר \| ≠ רוּחַ

body \= the physical body of humans, not including the mind and the inner person

Happen \| verb nif

Festivals

**Collocation:** חַג שָׁבֻעֹות

noun f · שׁבע

oath \= action by which humans or deities make a solemn statement often calling on a higher power to confirm that what is said is true or to punish the speaker if it turns out not to be true

שׁבח

שְׁבִי
======

noun m · שׁבה

taking captive, carrying off \= causative action by which people, animals, or objects are forcibly carried off; ► in the case of people, into slavery or exile

captive, prisoner \= a person or group that has been forcibly carried off to prison, into slavery, or resettlement

## שִׁבֹּלֶת (id: 007180000000000)

שִׁבֹּלֶת
=========

שִׁבֹּלֶת (H7641)

noun f

Flow

stream, flood \= process by which a liquid flows from one place to another

[PSA 69:3](https://ref.ly/Ps69:3) · [PSA 69:16](https://ref.ly/Ps69:16)

noun f

Plants

ear of grain \= uppermost part ◄ of a grain plant, ► which contains the seeds

Plants

branch meaning unsure; probably: \= part of a tree which grows out of the trunk or a bough

## שָׂבֵעַ (id: 006974000000000)

שָׂבֵעַ
=======

שָׂבֵעַ (H7649)

adj · שׂבע

Ingest \| ≠ רָעֵב

satiated \= process by which someone's desire for food is fulfilled

[1SA 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:5) · [PRO 27:7](https://ref.ly/Prov27:7)

Old

**Collocation:** שְׂבַע יָמִים

satisfied (with days) \> having lived long literally: satiated with days; hence: \= state in which humans have reached a very old age

[GEN 25:8](https://ref.ly/Gen25:8)1 · [GEN 35:29](https://ref.ly/Gen35:29) · [1CH 29:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:28) · [JOB 42:17](https://ref.ly/Job42:17)

Engage

satisfied, having (one's) fill (of something) literally: satiated (with an event); hence: \= process by which humans become more than fully engaged in a certain process or activity, which can be either good or bad

[DEU 33:23](https://ref.ly/Deut33:23) · [JOB 10:15](https://ref.ly/Job10:15) · [JOB 14:1](https://ref.ly/Job14:1)

Relief

satisfied literally: satiated; hence: \= process by which the desire of humans is fulfilled

[PRO 19:23](https://ref.ly/Prov19:23)

1 GEN 25:8 The word יָמִים is missing but obviously implied

Reinier de Blois

Vow \| verb hif

Vow \| verb nif

Revile \| verb nif

## שֶׁבֶר (id: 007192000000000)

שֶׁבֶר
======

שֶׁבֶר (H7667 · H7668 · H7669) — שֵׁבֶר

noun m · שׁבר

Impact

breaking, breach \= causative action or process by which humans or deities cause an object to be broken into two or more pieces; ≈ sometimes use figuratively to denote the sudden end of a certain condition or situation

[PSA 60:4](https://ref.ly/Ps60:4) · [ISA 30:13](https://ref.ly/Isa30:13) · [ISA 30:14](https://ref.ly/Isa30:14)

Impact

injury, fracture literally: to break; hence: \= causative action or process by which humans, deities, animals, or circumstances cause (other) humans or animals to be severely injured

[LEV 21:19](https://ref.ly/Lev21:19) · [LEV 21:19](https://ref.ly/Lev21:19) · [LEV 24:20](https://ref.ly/Lev24:20) · [LEV 24:20](https://ref.ly/Lev24:20) · [ISA 30:26](https://ref.ly/Isa30:26)

Grieve

**Collocation:** שֵׁבֶר רוּחַ · שֶׁבֶר בְּרוּחַ

to break (someone's) spirit \> to cause profound grief literally: breaking of (someone's) spirit; hence: \= causative process by which deities or circumstances cause humans to suffer profound grief

[PRO 15:4](https://ref.ly/Prov15:4) · [ISA 65:14](https://ref.ly/Isa65:14)

Know

interpretation literally: breaking; hence: \= action by which humans explain the meaning of an event that is hard to interpret, such as a dream

[JDG 7:15](https://ref.ly/Judg7:15)

Sounds

crashing sound literally: (sound of) breaking; hence: \= process by which a loud sound is heard, ◄ which is experienced as similar to the sound of something breaking

[JOB 41:17](https://ref.ly/Job41:17) · [ZEP 1:10](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:10)

Unsafe

disaster, destruction literally: breaking; hence: \= causative action by which humans, deities, or circumstances take away peace from a community by causing destruction, death, and suffering

[PRO 16:18](https://ref.ly/Prov16:18) · [PRO 17:19](https://ref.ly/Prov17:19) · [PRO 18:12](https://ref.ly/Prov18:12) · [ISA 1:28](https://ref.ly/Isa1:28) · [ISA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Isa15:5) · [ISA 51:19](https://ref.ly/Isa51:19) · [ISA 59:7](https://ref.ly/Isa59:7) · [ISA 60:18](https://ref.ly/Isa60:18) · [JER 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jer4:6) · [JER 4:20](https://ref.ly/Jer4:20) · [JER 4:20](https://ref.ly/Jer4:20) · [JER 6:1](https://ref.ly/Jer6:1) · [JER 6:14](https://ref.ly/Jer6:14) · [JER 8:11](https://ref.ly/Jer8:11) · [JER 8:21](https://ref.ly/Jer8:21) · [JER 10:19](https://ref.ly/Jer10:19) · [JER 14:17](https://ref.ly/Jer14:17) · [JER 30:12](https://ref.ly/Jer30:12) · [JER 30:15](https://ref.ly/Jer30:15) · [JER 48:3](https://ref.ly/Jer48:3) · [JER 48:5](https://ref.ly/Jer48:5) · [JER 50:22](https://ref.ly/Jer50:22) · [JER 51:54](https://ref.ly/Jer51:54) · [LAM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Lam2:11) · [LAM 2:13](https://ref.ly/Lam2:13) · [LAM 3:47](https://ref.ly/Lam3:47) · [LAM 3:48](https://ref.ly/Lam3:48) · [LAM 4:10](https://ref.ly/Lam4:10) · [EZK 32:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:9) · [AMO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Amos6:6) · [NAM 3:19](https://ref.ly/Nah3:19)

Birth \| verb hif

to bring about birth literally: to break (the womb); hence: \= causative process by which deities cause women to give birth

End

## שִׁבָּרֹון (id: 007193000000000)

שִׁבָּרֹון
==========

שִׁבָּרֹון (H7670)

noun m · שׁבר

Weak

**Collocation:** שִׁבְרֹון מָתְנַיִם

breaking (someone's) loins \> weak legs literally: breaking of (someone's) loins; hence: \= causative process by which deities or circumstances cause humans to feel weak and unable to stand firm; ≈ associated with strong fear

[EZK 21:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:11)1

Unsafe

disaster, destruction literally: breaking; hence: \= causative action by which humans, deities, or circumstances take away peace from a community by causing destruction, death, and suffering

[JER 17:18](https://ref.ly/Jer17:18)

שַׁבָּתֹון
==========

שַׁבָּתֹון (H7677)

noun m · שׁבת

Non\-Engage

(time of) solemn rest \= a time of solemn rest, like a sabbath or certain feast days, on which no work is to be done

[EXO 16:23](https://ref.ly/Exod16:23) · [EXO 31:15](https://ref.ly/Exod31:15) · [EXO 35:2](https://ref.ly/Exod35:2) · [LEV 16:31](https://ref.ly/Lev16:31) · [LEV 23:3](https://ref.ly/Lev23:3) · [LEV 23:24](https://ref.ly/Lev23:24) · [LEV 23:32](https://ref.ly/Lev23:32) · [LEV 23:39](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39) · [LEV 23:39](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39) · [LEV 25:4](https://ref.ly/Lev25:4) · [LEV 25:5](https://ref.ly/Lev25:5)

## שׂגב (id: 006982000000000)

שׂגב
====

שׂגב (H7682)

verb · מִשְׂגָּב

High \| verb nif ptc.pass

high \= state in which an object is of great or more than average height; ≈ associated with security

[PRO 18:11](https://ref.ly/Prov18:11) · [ISA 30:13](https://ref.ly/Isa30:13)

Conflict \| verb pi

to raise up against, to stir up against literally: to make (someone) high (against someone else); hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to engage in a conflict with someone else

[ISA 9:10](https://ref.ly/Isa9:10)

Great \| verb nif, verb hif

to be exalted, to be great literally: to be high; hence: \= state in which deities have an elevated status

[JOB 36:22](https://ref.ly/Job36:22) · [ISA 2:11](https://ref.ly/Isa2:11) · [ISA 2:17](https://ref.ly/Isa2:17) · [ISA 33:5](https://ref.ly/Isa33:5)

Extraordinary \| verb nif

to be exalted, to be wonderful literally: to be high; hence: \= state in which a certain quality is so extraordinary that it cannot be fully appreciated by the human mind

[PSA 139:6](https://ref.ly/Ps139:6) · [PSA 148:13](https://ref.ly/Ps148:13) · [ISA 12:4](https://ref.ly/Isa12:4)

Safe \| verb qal, verb nif

to be safe, to be secure literally: to be high; hence: \= state in which humans are safe from danger

[JOB 5:11](https://ref.ly/Job5:11) · [PRO 18:10](https://ref.ly/Prov18:10)

Safe \| verb pi, verb pu'al

to keep safe, to protect literally: to make high; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to be safe from danger

[PSA 20:2](https://ref.ly/Ps20:2) · [PSA 59:2](https://ref.ly/Ps59:2) · [PSA 69:30](https://ref.ly/Ps69:30) · [PSA 91:14](https://ref.ly/Ps91:14) · [PSA 107:41](https://ref.ly/Ps107:41) · [PRO 29:25](https://ref.ly/Prov29:25)

Strong \| verb qal

to be high \> to be strong literally: to be high; hence: \= state in which a town is situated at a relatively high elevation, ≈ which makes it unattainable and therefore strong

[DEU 2:36](https://ref.ly/Deut2:36)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 18:11; Isaiah 30:13; Isaiah 9:10; Job 36:22; Isaiah 2:11; Isaiah 2:17; Isaiah 33:5; Psalms 139:6; Psalms 148:13; Isaiah 12:4; Job 5:11; Proverbs 18:10; Psalms 20:2; Psalms 59:2; Psalms 69:30; Psalms 91:14; Psalms 107:41; Proverbs 29:25; Deuteronomy 2:36

## שׂדד (id: 006988000000000)

שׂדד
====

שׂדד (H7702)

verb

Impact \| verb pi \| ≈ חרשׁ

to break up (soil), to plow, to harrow \= causative action by which someone breaks the soil with the help of a plow, pulled by domestic animals; ► to make it ready for cultivation; ≈ usually regarded as a strenuous task which requires much energy

[JOB 39:10](https://ref.ly/Job39:10) · [ISA 28:24](https://ref.ly/Isa28:24) · [HOS 10:11](https://ref.ly/Hos10:11)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Job 39:10; Isaiah 28:24; Hosea 10:11


## שׁדד (id: 007216000000000)

שׁדד
====

שׁדד (H7703 · H7736)

verb · שֹׁד

Impact \| Dead \| Possess \| Unsafe \| verb qal \| ≈ ערף

to ravage, to devastate, to lay waste, to plunder \= action by which humans or wild animals attack other humans and kill, destroy and steal in the process

## שִׁדָּה (id: 007217000000000)

שִׁדָּה
=======

שִׁדָּה (H7705)

noun f · שַׁד

People

**Collocation:** שִׁדָּה וְשִׁדֹּות

many concubines exact meaning unknown; possibly: literally: breast and breasts; hence: \= a significant number of female companions, primarily for the purpose of sex

[ECC 2:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:8) · [ECC 2:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:8)

noun f · שׁדד

Containers \| ≈ שִׁדָּה וְשִׁדֹּות

many coffers, many chests exact meaning unknown; possibly: literally: coffer and coffers; hence: \= a significant number of containers for storing wealth

[ECC 2:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:8) · [ECC 2:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:8)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Ecclesiastes 2:8


## שָׂדֶה (id: 006989000000000)

שָׂדֶה
======

שָׂדֶה (H7704) — שָׂדַי

noun m · שְׂדֵה צֹפִים

Landscapes \| ≈ אֶרֶץ · חוּץ · נָוָה \| ≠ אֹהֶל · בַּיִת · מַחֲנֶה · עִיר

countryside, bush, uninhabited land \= a piece of land generally uninhabited by human beings, but where one can find wild animals and plants and trees; consists of grasslands, fields, desert, or forest; can be used for farming, pasturing animals, and hunting; often used in contrast to inhabited land such as cities or encampments.

Landscapes \| ≈ אֲחֻזָּה · אֶרֶץ · חֶלְקָה · יָגֵב · כֶּרֶם · כַּרְמֶל · מִקְשָׁה · שְׁדֵמָה \| ≠ בַּיִת · עִיר

field, landholding, property \= a piece of land outside of inhabited areas, which is the property of individuals, generally used for cultivating food \- trees, grain, and vegetables; can be bought and sold; property which can be transferred from one person to another

Angry \| verb hif

Associate \| verb hif, verb qal, verb pi

to turn (someone) back (to someone) literally: to turn (someone to someone else); hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities cause other humans to associate with deities, often to look for help and support

[2CH 19:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:4) · [2CH 24:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:19) · [NEH 9:26](https://ref.ly/Neh9:26) · [ISA 49:5](https://ref.ly/Isa49:5) · [LAM 5:21](https://ref.ly/Lam5:21)

Dissociate \| verb qal, verb hif

to turn away (from someone) literally: to turn away (from someone); hence: \= action by which humans or deities stop associating with other humans or deities

[DEU 23:15](https://ref.ly/Deut23:15) · [EZK 14:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:6)

End \| verb qal

to turn away, to come to an end, to cease, to subside literally: (an event) turns away (from someone); hence: \= process by which a certain condition comes to an end

[GEN 27:44](https://ref.ly/Gen27:44) · [GEN 27:45](https://ref.ly/Gen27:45) · [NUM 25:4](https://ref.ly/Num25:4) · [2CH 12:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:12) · [2CH 29:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:10) · [2CH 30:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:8) · [JOB 14:13](https://ref.ly/Job14:13) · [PRO 25:10](https://ref.ly/Prov25:10) · [ISA 5:25](https://ref.ly/Isa5:25) · [ISA 9:11](https://ref.ly/Isa9:11) · [ISA 9:16](https://ref.ly/Isa9:16) · [ISA 9:20](https://ref.ly/Isa9:20) · [ISA 10:4](https://ref.ly/Isa10:4) · [ISA 12:1](https://ref.ly/Isa12:1) · [JER 2:35](https://ref.ly/Jer2:35) · [JER 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jer4:8) · [JER 23:20](https://ref.ly/Jer23:20) · [JER 30:24](https://ref.ly/Jer30:24) · [DAN 9:16](https://ref.ly/Dan9:16) · [HOS 14:5](https://ref.ly/Hos14:5)

End \| verb hif

to turn back, to revoke literally: to cause (an event) to turn away (from someone else); hence: \= action by which deities cause a condition in which they are personally involved to come to an end

[JOB 9:13](https://ref.ly/Job9:13) · [PSA 78:38](https://ref.ly/Ps78:38) · [PRO 24:18](https://ref.ly/Prov24:18) · [AMO 1:3](https://ref.ly/Amos1:3) · [AMO 1:6](https://ref.ly/Amos1:6) · [AMO 1:8](https://ref.ly/Amos1:8) · [AMO 1:9](https://ref.ly/Amos1:9) · [AMO 1:11](https://ref.ly/Amos1:11) · [AMO 1:13](https://ref.ly/Amos1:13) · [AMO 2:1](https://ref.ly/Amos2:1) · [AMO 2:4](https://ref.ly/Amos2:4) · [AMO 2:6](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6)

End \| verb hif

to (cause to) turn back, to cause to cease, to avert, to revoke literally: to cause (an event) to turn away (from someone else); hence: \= causative action by which humans cause a certain condition in which they are not personally involved to come to an end

[NUM 23:20](https://ref.ly/Num23:20) · [NUM 25:11](https://ref.ly/Num25:11) · [EZR 10:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:14) · [PSA 106:23](https://ref.ly/Ps106:23) · [PRO 15:1](https://ref.ly/Prov15:1) · [PRO 29:8](https://ref.ly/Prov29:8) · [ISA 28:6](https://ref.ly/Isa28:6) · [ISA 43:13](https://ref.ly/Isa43:13) · [JER 2:24](https://ref.ly/Jer2:24) · [JER 18:20](https://ref.ly/Jer18:20)

Engage \| verb qal

to turn (back) to, to engage (again) in literally: to turn (back) to (an activity); hence: \= action by which humans engage in an activity; in most cases this is an activity in which they were engaged before

[PSA 85:9](https://ref.ly/Ps85:9) · [PSA 116:7](https://ref.ly/Ps116:7) · [PRO 1:23](https://ref.ly/Prov1:23) · [ECC 1:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:6) · [ECC 1:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:7) · [ISA 23:17](https://ref.ly/Isa23:17) · [JER 8:4](https://ref.ly/Jer8:4) · [JER 8:6](https://ref.ly/Jer8:6) · [JER 11:10](https://ref.ly/Jer11:10)

Engage \| verb hif \| ≈ נכה

**Collocation:** שׁוב יָד

to take action (against) literally: to turn (one's) hand (against someone); hence: \= action by which deities take action against humans, usually involving acts of punishment

[PSA 81:15](https://ref.ly/Ps81:15) · [ISA 1:25](https://ref.ly/Isa1:25) · [EZK 38:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:12) · [ZEC 13:7](https://ref.ly/Zech13:7)

Engage \| verb hif

to turn (someone back) to literally: to cause to turn back (to an activity); hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to engage in an activity; in most cases this is an activity in which they were engaged before

[NEH 9:29](https://ref.ly/Neh9:29)

Engage \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב רֶגֶל

to engage (in) literally: to turn (one's) feet (to an activity); hence: \= action by which humans engage in a certain activity

[PSA 119:59](https://ref.ly/Ps119:59)

Non\-Engage \| verb qal, verb hif

to turn back from, to give up, to retire from literally: to turn (from an activity); hence: \= action by which humans or deities stop being engaged in a certain activity

Non\-Engage \| verb hif

to make (someone) give up, to hinder literally: to cause (someone else) to turn (from an activity); hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities cause (other) humans or deities to stop being engaged in a certain activity

[JOB 9:12](https://ref.ly/Job9:12) · [JOB 11:10](https://ref.ly/Job11:10) · [JOB 23:13](https://ref.ly/Job23:13) · [ISA 44:25](https://ref.ly/Isa44:25) · [JER 23:22](https://ref.ly/Jer23:22) · [MAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6)

Non\-Engage \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב פָּנֶה

to give up literally: to turn (one's) face (from an activity); hence: \= action by which humans stop being engaged in a certain activity

[EZK 14:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:6)

Non\-Engage \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב יָד · שׁוב יָמִין

to stay one's hand, to abstain (from) literally: to turn (one's) hand (from an activity); hence: \= action by which humans keep themselves from engaging in a certain activity

[LAM 2:3](https://ref.ly/Lam2:3) · [LAM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Lam2:8) · [EZK 18:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:8) · [EZK 18:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:17)5 · [EZK 20:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:22)

Non\-Engage \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב רֶגֶל

to refrain (from) literally: to turn (one's) feet (from an activity); hence: \= action by which humans keep themselves from engaging in a certain activity

[ISA 58:13](https://ref.ly/Isa58:13)

Non\-Engage \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב סֵפֶר

to revoke literally: to cause a letter to come back; hence: \= causative action by which humans declare a letter that they have sent null and void, as if it has not been sent

[EST 8:5](https://ref.ly/Esth8:5) · [EST 8:8](https://ref.ly/Esth8:8)

Fail \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב צוּר חֶרֶב

to cause (someone) to fail in battle literally: to turn the blade of (someone's) sword; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to fail in battle

[PSA 89:44](https://ref.ly/Ps89:44)

Move \| verb qal

to move backwards \= process by which something moves backwards

[2KI 20:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:9) · [2KI 20:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:10) · [ISA 38:8](https://ref.ly/Isa38:8)

Move \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁוב אָחֹור

to move backwards literally: to move back; hence: \= process by which humans move backwards without turning around

[LAM 1:8](https://ref.ly/Lam1:8)

Move \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב אָחֹור

to push backwards literally: to cause (someone) to move back; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to stop advancing and move backwards instead

[PSA 44:11](https://ref.ly/Ps44:11) · [LAM 1:13](https://ref.ly/Lam1:13)

Move \| verb qal

to turn back literally: to turn; hence: \= action by which humans stop moving into a certain direction and start moving back into the direction they came from

to take back, to bring back, to return, to put back, to restore (something) literally: to turn; hence: \= causative action by which animals, humans, deities cause (other) animals, humans, or objects to move back to a location where they have been before

Move \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב פָּנֶה

to turn back (towards) literally: to turn (one's) face back towards (something); hence: \= action by which humans decide to turn back and return to the place thy came from

[DAN 11:19](https://ref.ly/Dan11:19)

Move \| verb pi

to cause to roam exact meaning unsure; possible: \= causative action by which humans in authority cause other humans move from place to place in a certain area

[JER 50:6](https://ref.ly/Jer50:6)

Act \| verb pi

to lead astray, to mislead literally: to cause to turn; hence: \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to pursue a wrong course of events

[ISA 47:10](https://ref.ly/Isa47:10)

Alive \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב נֶפֶשׁ מִן־שַׁחַת

to bring (someone) back to life literally: to bring back (someone's) life from the pit; hence: \= causative action by which deities brings humans back to life

[JOB 33:30](https://ref.ly/Job33:30)

Alive \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב

to bring (someone) back (to life) literally: to bring (someone) back; hence: \= causative action by which humans bring (other) humans back (from sheol)

[2SA 12:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:23)

Attribute \| verb qal

to be restored literally: to come back; hence: \= process by which an object is restored to its former condition

[EXO 4:7](https://ref.ly/Exod4:7) · [1KI 13:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:6) · [1KI 13:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:6) · [2KI 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:10) · [2KI 5:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:14) · [EZK 16:55](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:55) · [EZK 16:55](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:55) · [EZK 16:55](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:55)

Blow \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב רוּחַ

to recover (one's) breath \= process by which humans get the rest needed in order to be able to breathe evenly

[JOB 9:18](https://ref.ly/Job9:18)

Control \| verb hif

to restore (to one's control) literally: to cause to return to oneself; hence: \= action by which humans regain control over something

[2KI 13:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:25)

Control \| verb hif

to restore (to someone's control) literally: to cause to return (to someone); hence: \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to regain control over something

Dead \| verb qal

**Collocation:** עָפָר שׁוב עַל־אֶרֶץ

dust returns to the earth, to pass away, to die literally: dust returns to the earth; hence: \= process by which humans die and their body decomposes and they become one with the earth

[ECC 12:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:7)

Dead \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁוב אֶל־אֲדָמָה · שׁוב לַאֲדָמָה

to return to soil, to pass away, to die literally: to return to the soil; hence: \= process by which humans die and their body decomposes and they become one with the earth

[GEN 3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:19) · [PSA 146:4](https://ref.ly/Ps146:4)

Dead \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁוב אֶל־עָפָר · שׁוב עַל־עָפָר

to return to dust, to pass away, to die literally: to return to dust; hence: \= process by which humans die and their body decomposes and they become one with the earth

[GEN 3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:19) · [JOB 34:15](https://ref.ly/Job34:15) · [PSA 104:29](https://ref.ly/Ps104:29) · [ECC 3:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:20)

Dead \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב עַד דַּכָּא

to make (someone) return to dust, to cause (someone) to die literally: to bring back until dust; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to die so that their body decomposes and they become one with the earth

[PSA 90:3](https://ref.ly/Ps90:3) · [PSA 90:3](https://ref.ly/Ps90:3)

Dead \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב אֶל־עָפָר

to make (someone) return to dust, to cause (someone) to die literally: to bring back to dust; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to die so that their body decomposes and they become one with the earth

[JOB 10:9](https://ref.ly/Job10:9)

Dead \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב מָוֶת

to bring to death literally: to cause (someone) to return to death; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to die

[JOB 30:23](https://ref.ly/Job30:23)

Different \| verb qal

to change, to turn into \= process by which an object changes into something else

[ISA 29:17](https://ref.ly/Isa29:17) · [MIC 1:7](https://ref.ly/Mic1:7)

Engage \| verb qal

to return literally: to come back; hence: \= process by which a certain former relation is restored

[1KI 12:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:26)

Engage \| verb qal

to restore literally: to come back; hence: \= action by which humans reengage in an activity that they had been engaging in before

[PSA 78:34](https://ref.ly/Ps78:34)

Engage \| verb hif

to restore literally: to bring back; hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities restore a certain former condition or relation

Fail \| verb qal

to return empty literally: to return (without having reached its goal); \= process by which an object or event fails to reach its intended goal

to return, to come back, to happen again literally: to come back; hence: \= process by which a certain event happens once more

[LEV 14:43](https://ref.ly/Lev14:43) · [PSA 94:15](https://ref.ly/Ps94:15)

Happen \| verb hif

to bring back, to restore literally: to bring back; hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities cause an event to take place that has happened before

[DEU 28:60](https://ref.ly/Deut28:60) · [PSA 51:14](https://ref.ly/Ps51:14)

Happen \| verb hif

to repay, to pay back, to punish, to reward literally: to send back (to someone); hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities case (other) humans or deities to receive the appropriate return for their actions, which can be either good or bad

Chastise \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁוב אֶל־חֵיק

to pay back literally: to send back to (someone's) bosom; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to receive the appropriate return for their evil actions, which are mostly evil

Chastise \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁוב בְּרֹאשׁ · שׁוב עַל־רֹאשׁ

to be repaid, to be paid back, to be punished literally: to come back on (someone's) head; hence: \= process by which humans receive the appropriate return for their actions, which are mostly evil

**Collocation:** שׁוב אֶל־רֹאשׁ · שׁוב בְּרֹאשׁ · שׁוב עַל־רֹאשׁ

to repay, to pay back, to punish literally: to send back on (someone's) head; hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities case (other) humans to receive the appropriate return for their actions, which are mostly evil

Whole \| verb qal

to be restored literally: to return (to a former state); hence: \= process by which an object is restored to its original state

[JOB 33:25](https://ref.ly/Job33:25)

Whole \| verb qal, verb pi, verb hif

to restore literally: to bring back (something into its former state); hence: \= causative action by which humans restore an object to its original state

Know \| Think \| verb hif

to take to heart, to keep in mind, to call to mind literally: to bring (a matter) back to (one's) heart; hence: \= action by which humans store information in their minds and continue to reflect on it

Possess \| verb qal

Possess \| verb hif

Relief \| verb hif

Role \| verb hif

## שׁוה (id: 007239000000000)

שׁוה
====

שׁוה (H7737 · H7738 · A7739)

verb · יִשְׁוָה · יִשְׁוִי

Similar \| verb qal, verb nitp, verb pi

to be alike, to be like, to compare with \= process by which an object or event is considered similar to another object or event

[EST 7:4](https://ref.ly/Esth7:4) · [PRO 3:15](https://ref.ly/Prov3:15) · [PRO 8:11](https://ref.ly/Prov8:11) · [PRO 26:4](https://ref.ly/Prov26:4) · [PRO 27:15](https://ref.ly/Prov27:15) · [ISA 40:25](https://ref.ly/Isa40:25) · [HOS 10:1](https://ref.ly/Hos10:1)1

Similar \| verb pi, verb pa, verb hitpa'al

to make like \= causative action by which humans or deities cause objects to become similar to another objects

[2SA 22:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:34) · [PSA 18:34](https://ref.ly/Ps18:34) · [DAN 3:29](https://ref.ly/Dan3:29) · [DAN 5:21](https://ref.ly/Dan5:21)

Chastise

to be paid back (for something) literally: to be made equal; hence: \= process by which humans are punished in a way that is proportional to the offense

[JOB 33:27](https://ref.ly/Job33:27)

Compare \| verb hif \| ≈ דמה

to compare \= action by which humans measure the similarity or dissimilarity between two entities

[ISA 46:5](https://ref.ly/Isa46:5) · [LAM 2:13](https://ref.ly/Lam2:13)

Patient \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁוה נֶפֶשׁ

to cause to be quiet, to cause to be at ease, to calm literally: to make (one's) inner person equal; hence: \= action by which humans make an effort to calm down emotions such as anger and discontent

[PSA 131:2](https://ref.ly/Ps131:2)

Shape \| verb pi

to level, to make smooth literally: to make (something) equal; hence: \= causative action by which humans use a tool to make a rough surface more smooth

[ISA 28:25](https://ref.ly/Isa28:25)

Well \| verb qal

to be acceptable, to be tolerable literally: to be equal (to someone); hence: \= state in which a situation is acceptable to someone and does not need to be altered

[EST 3:8](https://ref.ly/Esth3:8) · [EST 5:13](https://ref.ly/Esth5:13)

verb

Engage \| verb pi

to grant literally: to place (a quality or activity) upon (someone); hence: \= process by which humans receive a certain quality from God, or become the beneficiary of a divine action

[PSA 21:6](https://ref.ly/Ps21:6) · [PSA 89:20](https://ref.ly/Ps89:20)3

Think \| verb pi

to be aware (of), to think (of) literally: to place (before oneself); hence: \= action by which humans constantly keep a certain object or event at the back of their minds

[PSA 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ps16:8) · [PSA 119:30](https://ref.ly/Ps119:30)

verb

Shout \| verb pi

to cry for help \= action by which humans raise their voice ◄ in times of hardship ► hoping to received help from God or someone else

[ISA 38:13](https://ref.ly/Isa38:13)4

verb

Stage \| verb pi

to produce, to yield meaning unsure; possibly: \= process by which a plant produces fruits

[HOS 10:1](https://ref.ly/Hos10:1)1

[JOB 8:21](https://ref.ly/Job8:21) · [PSA 126:2](https://ref.ly/Ps126:2) · [PRO 10:23](https://ref.ly/Prov10:23) · [PRO 14:13](https://ref.ly/Prov14:13) · [ECC 2:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:2) · [ECC 7:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:3) · [ECC 7:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:6) · [ECC 10:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:19)

Revile \| ≈ מְחִתָּה · נְגִינָה

laughingstock literally: (object of) laughter; hence: \= a person or group who has to endure lack of respect from others, often accompanied by laughter

שׁחח

verb · שַׁח

Low \| verb qal

Grieve \| verb qal

**Collocation:** נֶפֶשׁ שׁחח

to be in distress literally: (one's) self is low; hence: \= process by which humans experience grief

[LAM 3:20](https://ref.ly/Lam3:20)

End \| verb qal \| ≈ שׁפל \| ≠ גֵּאֶה · גַּבְהוּת · רוּם

to be brought down, to be humbled literally: to be brought low; hence: \= process by which human pride is brought to an end, which often becomes visible in their attitude and appearance

[ISA 2:11](https://ref.ly/Isa2:11) · [ISA 2:17](https://ref.ly/Isa2:17)

Impact \| verb hif \| ≈ שׁפל

to bring down literally: to bring low; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause high and imposing objects to become low ◄ by making them fall or collapse; ≈ associated with a loss of status

[ISA 25:12](https://ref.ly/Isa25:12)

Insignificant \| verb hif \| ≈ שׁפל

to humble, to humiliate literally: to bring low; hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to lose their high status within a community

[ISA 26:5](https://ref.ly/Isa26:5)

Insignificant \| verb qal \| ≈ שׁפל

to be humbled, to be humiliated literally: to be brought low; hence: \= process by which humans lose their high status within a community

[PSA 107:39](https://ref.ly/Ps107:39) · [ISA 2:9](https://ref.ly/Isa2:9) · [ISA 5:15](https://ref.ly/Isa5:15)

Lax \| verb nif

to become low, to fade away literally: to become low; hence: \= process by which an event is reduced in intensity

[ECC 12:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:4) · [ISA 29:4](https://ref.ly/Isa29:4)

Stance \| verb qal

to sink down literally: to become low; hence: \= process by which humans are unable to maintain a standing position and are forced down to the ground

[JOB 9:13](https://ref.ly/Job9:13) · [PSA 10:10](https://ref.ly/Ps10:10)

Stance \| verb qal

to lie down, to crouch literally: to make oneself low; hence: \= action by which animals assume a horizontal position

[JOB 38:40](https://ref.ly/Job38:40)

Bow \| verb qal \| ≈ חוה

to bow down literally: to lower oneself; hence: \= action by which humans bend the upper body as a sign of mourning or submission

[PSA 35:14](https://ref.ly/Ps35:14) · [PSA 38:7](https://ref.ly/Ps38:7) · [PRO 14:19](https://ref.ly/Prov14:19) · [ISA 60:14](https://ref.ly/Isa60:14)

====

verb · מִשְׂחָק · שְׂחֹוק

Rejoice \| verb qal \| ≠ בכה

to laugh, to smile \= action by which humans give expression to a friendly attitude, joy and amusement with the gestures and sounds that normally accompany it; ≈ this is normally regarded as a good thing, especially when done with restraint, whereas a lack of restraint is often associated with foolishness

Rejoice \| verb pi, verb qal \| ≈ רקד

to make merry, to play, to joke \= action by which humans engage in activities associated with joy and contentment by leaping, dancing, celebrating, and even engaging in games

## שׁטף (id: 007318000000000)

שׁטף
====

שׁטף (H7857)

verb · שֶׁטֶף · שֶׁצֶף

Flow \| verb qal

to flow, to overflow \= process by which water flows with great strength and potentially destructive force; ≈ often used as an image for large quantities or overwhelming destruction, military strength, or suffering

[2CH 32:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:4) · [PSA 69:3](https://ref.ly/Ps69:3) · [PSA 69:16](https://ref.ly/Ps69:16) · [PSA 78:20](https://ref.ly/Ps78:20) · [PSA 124:4](https://ref.ly/Ps124:4) · [ISA 8:8](https://ref.ly/Isa8:8) · [ISA 28:2](https://ref.ly/Isa28:2) · [ISA 28:17](https://ref.ly/Isa28:17) · [ISA 30:28](https://ref.ly/Isa30:28) · [ISA 43:2](https://ref.ly/Isa43:2) · [ISA 66:12](https://ref.ly/Isa66:12) · [JER 47:2](https://ref.ly/Jer47:2) · [JER 47:2](https://ref.ly/Jer47:2) · [EZK 13:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:11) · [EZK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:13) · [EZK 38:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:22)

Move \| verb qal

to sweep through literally: to overflow; hence: \= action by which humans or animals move through an area with an irresistable force

[JER 8:6](https://ref.ly/Jer8:6) · [DAN 11:10](https://ref.ly/Dan11:10) · [DAN 11:40](https://ref.ly/Dan11:40)

Intense \| verb qal

to overwhelm literally: to overflow; hence: \= state in which an event happens with great intensity, ◄ compared to an overwhelming flood

[ISA 10:22](https://ref.ly/Isa10:22) · [ISA 28:15](https://ref.ly/Isa28:15) · [ISA 28:18](https://ref.ly/Isa28:18)

Non\-Exist \| verb nif, verb qal

to be swept away literally: to be overflown; hence: \= causative process by which large groups of humans are caused to disappear, ◄ as if they were swept away by a flood

[DAN 11:22](https://ref.ly/Dan11:22) · [DAN 11:26](https://ref.ly/Dan11:26)

Non\-Exist \| verb qal

to wash away, to sweep away literally: overflow; hence: \= causative process by which large quantities of water remove objects from their location

[JOB 14:19](https://ref.ly/Job14:19)

Clean \| verb qal, verb nif, verb pu'al \| ≈ מרק

to wash, to rinse, to wash away to overflow; hence: \= causative action by which humans use water to remove dirt from an object

[LEV 6:21](https://ref.ly/Lev6:21) · [LEV 15:11](https://ref.ly/Lev15:11) · [LEV 15:12](https://ref.ly/Lev15:12) · [1KI 22:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:38) · [EZK 16:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:9)

Quench

to drown, to extinguish, to sweep away literally: to overflow; hence: \= causative process by which large quantities of water cause a fire to come to an end

[SNG 8:7](https://ref.ly/Song8:7)

Reinier de Blois

Bear \| verb qal

to place (responsibility on someone) \> to lay upon, to bring upon, to impose upon, to charge with, to commit to \= causative action by which humans or deities hold (other) humans responsible for an event that has taken place or that is to take place in the future

Birth \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים בָּנִים

to have children literally: to put children; hence: \= causative action by which humans bring forth children

Chastise \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים יָד

to inflict punishment literally: to lay (one's) hand (on someone); hence: \= causative action by which deities inflict punishment on nations, ◄ as if striking them with their hand

[EZK 39:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:21)

Different \| verb qal, verb hif, verb hitpe'el

to turn into, to change \= causative action by which humans or deities make objects or (other) humans different from what they were before

Dwell \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים בֵּית

to make (one's) home literally: to place (one's) home; hence: \= action by which humans settle in a certain place with the purpose of making it their home

[PRO 30:26](https://ref.ly/Prov30:26)

Dwell \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים שֵׁם

to establish (one's) name (somewhere) literally: to put (one's) name (somewhere); hence: \= causative action by which deities link their name to a certain location by declaring it their dwelling\-place

[DEU 12:5](https://ref.ly/Deut12:5) · [DEU 12:21](https://ref.ly/Deut12:21) · [DEU 14:24](https://ref.ly/Deut14:24) · [1KI 9:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:3) · [1KI 11:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:36) · [1KI 14:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:21) · [2KI 21:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:4) · [2KI 21:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:7) · [2CH 6:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:20) · [2CH 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:13) · [2CH 33:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:7)

Exist \| verb qal

to make \= causative action by which deities call objects to existence

Exist \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים שֵׁם וּשְׁאֵרית

to leave (someone) name and descendants literally: to put name and remnant; hence: \= causative action by which humans do what is needed to ensure that they (or others) have living descendants to perpetuate their name

[GEN 45:7](https://ref.ly/Gen45:7) · [2SA 14:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:7)

Hold \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים יָדַיִם

to seize literally: to put (one's) hands (on someone); hence: \= action by which humans seize other humans in order to take them into custody

[2KI 11:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:16) · [2CH 23:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:15)

Great \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים כִּסֵּא

to give a certain status literally: to put (someone's) seat (somewhere); hence: \= causative action by which humans confer a certain status upon other humans

[EST 3:1](https://ref.ly/Esth3:1)

Great \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים שֵׁם

to make a name (for oneself) literally: to put a name (for oneself); hence: \= action by which humans make an effort to increase their status by certain actions

[2SA 7:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:23) · [1CH 17:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:21)

Happen \| verb qal

to cause to happen \= causative action by which humans or deities cause an event to happen ◄ as if it were an object placed in a certain position

Happen \| verb qal

to pay back, to repay, to reward \= action by which humans act towards a person in a way that has consequences and can result either in retribution or a reward

[PSA 109:5](https://ref.ly/Ps109:5)

Hear \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים בְּאָזְנַיִם

to pronounce literally: to put (something) in (someone's) ears; hence: \= causative action by which humans speak words in the hearing of other humans

[EXO 17:14](https://ref.ly/Exod17:14)

Know \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים לְפָנֶה

to set before (someone) \> to inform (someone) literally: to place before (someone); hence: \= causative action by which humans pass on a message to other humans ◄ as if it were an object placed in front of them

[EXO 19:7](https://ref.ly/Exod19:7) · [EXO 21:1](https://ref.ly/Exod21:1) · [DEU 4:44](https://ref.ly/Deut4:44)

Name \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים שֵׁם

to name literally: to put (someone's) name; hence: \= causative action by which humans assign a name to other humans

[NUM 6:27](https://ref.ly/Num6:27) · [JDG 8:31](https://ref.ly/Judg8:31) · [2KI 17:34](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:34) · [NEH 9:7](https://ref.ly/Neh9:7) · [DAN 1:7](https://ref.ly/Dan1:7) · [DAN 1:7](https://ref.ly/Dan1:7) · [DAN 5:12](https://ref.ly/Dan5:12)

Praise \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים כָּבֹוד

to give glory, to glorify literally: to put glory; hence: \= causative action by which humans express their respect for a deity with words and actions

[JOS 7:19](https://ref.ly/Josh7:19) · [PSA 66:2](https://ref.ly/Ps66:2) · [ISA 42:12](https://ref.ly/Isa42:12)

Right \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים דֶּרֶךְ

to improve (one's) way literally: to put (one's) way; hence: \= action by which humans change their mode of behavior

[PSA 50:23](https://ref.ly/Ps50:23)

Role \| verb qal

to place (in a position) \> to appoint \= causative action by which humans or deities give (other) humans a certain task or responsibility

See \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים עֵינַיִם

See \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים פָּנֶה

Speak \| verb qal

Strong \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂים בְּיָד

## שַׁיִן (id: 007335000000000)

שַׁיִן
======

שַׁיִן (H7890)

noun m · שׁין

Body Products

urine \= the pale yellowish liquid passed from the bladder ◄ of animals and human beings; ► used as food in times of extreme hardship; ≈ this word appears to have been considered indecent because it has been replaced by a euphemism in all passages where it is found

## שׁין (id: 007334000000000)

שׁין
====

שׁין (H8366) — שׁתן

verb · שַׁיִן

People \| verb hif ptc.act

**Collocation:** מַשְׁתִּין בְּקִיר

male literally: person who urinates against the wall; hence: \= male human; ≈ used exclusively in contexts where this person is the victim of aggression

שׁית
====

שׁית (H7896 · H8371)

verb · שִׁית

Space \| verb qal

to take position \= action by which individuals or groups of humans place themselves in a certain position or configuration

[JOB 10:20](https://ref.ly/Job10:20) · [JOB 38:11](https://ref.ly/Job38:11)2 · [PSA 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ps3:7) · [ISA 22:7](https://ref.ly/Isa22:7) · [ISA 22:7](https://ref.ly/Isa22:7)

Space \| verb qal

to place, to put, to lay, to set, to keep \= causative action by which humans or deities cause objects or (other) humans to be located in a certain place

[GEN 30:40](https://ref.ly/Gen30:40) · [GEN 30:40](https://ref.ly/Gen30:40) · [GEN 46:4](https://ref.ly/Gen46:4) · [GEN 48:14](https://ref.ly/Gen48:14) · [GEN 48:17](https://ref.ly/Gen48:17) · [RUT 3:15](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:15) · [RUT 4:16](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:16) · [1SA 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:8) · [2SA 19:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:29) · [JOB 22:24](https://ref.ly/Job22:24) · [JOB 30:1](https://ref.ly/Job30:1) · [PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6) · [PSA 21:4](https://ref.ly/Ps21:4) · [PSA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Ps49:15) · [PSA 73:18](https://ref.ly/Ps73:18) · [PSA 84:4](https://ref.ly/Ps84:4) · [PSA 88:7](https://ref.ly/Ps88:7) · [PSA 132:11](https://ref.ly/Ps132:11) · [PSA 140:6](https://ref.ly/Ps140:6) · [PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3) · [JER 3:19](https://ref.ly/Jer3:19)

Dress \| verb qal

to put on (ornaments) \= action by which humans put ornaments on parts of their body

[EXO 33:4](https://ref.ly/Exod33:4)

Different \| verb qal

to turn into, to change \= causative action by which humans or deities make objects or (other) humans different from what they were before

[PSA 21:10](https://ref.ly/Ps21:10) · [PSA 83:12](https://ref.ly/Ps83:12) · [PSA 83:14](https://ref.ly/Ps83:14) · [PSA 84:7](https://ref.ly/Ps84:7) · [PSA 88:9](https://ref.ly/Ps88:9) · [PSA 110:1](https://ref.ly/Ps110:1) · [ISA 5:6](https://ref.ly/Isa5:6) · [ISA 16:3](https://ref.ly/Isa16:3) · [JER 2:15](https://ref.ly/Jer2:15) · [JER 13:16](https://ref.ly/Jer13:16) · [JER 22:6](https://ref.ly/Jer22:6) · [JER 50:3](https://ref.ly/Jer50:3) · [HOS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Hos2:5)

Exist \| verb qal

to make \= causative action by which deities call objects to existence

[GEN 4:25](https://ref.ly/Gen4:25)

Happen \| verb qal

to cause to happen \= causative action by which humans or deities cause an event to happen ◄ as if it were an object placed in a certain position

[GEN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15) · [EXO 10:1](https://ref.ly/Exod10:1) · [EXO 23:31](https://ref.ly/Exod23:31) · [JOB 38:36](https://ref.ly/Job38:36) · [PSA 9:21](https://ref.ly/Ps9:21) · [PSA 21:7](https://ref.ly/Ps21:7) · [PSA 104:20](https://ref.ly/Ps104:20) · [PRO 26:24](https://ref.ly/Prov26:24) · [ISA 15:9](https://ref.ly/Isa15:9) · [JER 51:39](https://ref.ly/Jer51:39) · [HOS 6:11](https://ref.ly/Hos6:11)

Bear \| verb qal, verb hof

to punish, to impose \= causative action by which humans or deities hold (other) humans responsible for an event that has taken place or that is to take place in the future

[EXO 21:22](https://ref.ly/Exod21:22) · [EXO 21:30](https://ref.ly/Exod21:30) · [EXO 21:30](https://ref.ly/Exod21:30) · [NUM 12:11](https://ref.ly/Num12:11)

Role \| verb qal

to place (in a position), to make, to appoint \= causative action by which humans or deities give (other) humans a certain task or responsibility

[GEN 41:33](https://ref.ly/Gen41:33) · [2SA 22:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:12) · [1KI 11:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:34) · [PSA 8:7](https://ref.ly/Ps8:7) · [PSA 18:12](https://ref.ly/Ps18:12) · [PSA 45:17](https://ref.ly/Ps45:17) · [ISA 26:1](https://ref.ly/Isa26:1)

Think \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית חֹק

to set a time literally: to set an allotment; hence: \= action by which deities determine a certain moment in the future for an event to take place

[JOB 14:13](https://ref.ly/Job14:13)

Control \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית יָד

to lay (one's) hand \> to impose (one's) authority (on someone) literally: to lay (one's) hand (on someone); hence: \= action by which deities impose their authority on humans

[JOB 9:33](https://ref.ly/Job9:33) · [PSA 139:5](https://ref.ly/Ps139:5)

Associate \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית יָד

to join hands (with someone) literally: to put (one's) hand (with someone); hence: \= action by which humans offer their help to someone else in order to undertake something together

[EXO 23:1](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1)

Think \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית לֵב

to take to heart, to be concerned about literally: to put (one's) heart on (something); hence: \= to process information in one's mind, often with the intention to act upon it later

[EXO 7:23](https://ref.ly/Exod7:23) · [1SA 4:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:20) · [2SA 13:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:20) · [JOB 7:17](https://ref.ly/Job7:17) · [PSA 48:14](https://ref.ly/Ps48:14) · [PSA 62:11](https://ref.ly/Ps62:11) · [PRO 22:17](https://ref.ly/Prov22:17) · [PRO 24:32](https://ref.ly/Prov24:32) · [PRO 27:23](https://ref.ly/Prov27:23) · [JER 31:21](https://ref.ly/Jer31:21)

Think \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית לְנֶגֶד

to set (one's) mind (on something) literally: to put before (oneself); hence: \= action by which humans or deities decide that something is of significant importance for them and needs to be dealt with

[PSA 90:8](https://ref.ly/Ps90:8) · [PSA 101:3](https://ref.ly/Ps101:3)

Confident \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית מַחְסֶה

to put their trust (in someone else) literally: to put as (one's) refuge; hence: \= action by which humans decide that they will put their trust in a deity ► in order to find protection or safety

[PSA 73:28](https://ref.ly/Ps73:28)

Think \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית עֵנַיִם

to plan literally: to set (one's) eyes (to do something); hence: \= action by which humans exercise their minds to determine what they will do

[PSA 17:11](https://ref.ly/Ps17:11)

Think \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית עֵצוֹת

to have cares (on one's mind) literally: \= to put counsel (in one's soul); hence: \= action by which humans reflect on issues that are of deep concern to them

[PSA 13:3](https://ref.ly/Ps13:3)

Speak \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית פֶּה בַּשָּׁמַיִם

to speak against heaven literally: to put (one's) mouth in heaven; hence, possibly: \= action by which humans speak inappropriately about God; ≈ associated with arrogance

[PSA 73:9](https://ref.ly/Ps73:9)

See \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית פָּנֶה

to face literally: to set (one's) face (in a certain direction); hence: \= action by which humans or deities fix their eyes in a certain direction, often with the purpose of going there or taking action against it

[NUM 24:1](https://ref.ly/Num24:1)

Stance \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית שְׁכֶם

to make prostrate literally: to make (someone) shoulder; hence, possibly: \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to lie flat on the ground ► so that they can place their foot on their shoulder or neck as a sign of victory3

[PSA 21:13](https://ref.ly/Ps21:13)

1 JOB 10:20 · JER 13:16 MT\-K ישׁית \| MT\-Q וְשִׁית 2 JOB 38:11 MT HOTTP\-C \| LXX HOTTP\-C 3 See Dahood in ABC

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Job 10:20; Job 38:11; Psalms 3:7; Isaiah 22:7; Genesis 30:40; Genesis 46:4; Genesis 48:14; Genesis 48:17; Ruth 3:15; Ruth 4:16; 1 Samuel 2:8; 2 Samuel 19:29; Job 22:24; Job 30:1; Psalms 12:6; Psalms 21:4; Psalms 49:15; Psalms 73:18; Psalms 84:4; Psalms 88:7; Psalms 132:11; Psalms 140:6; Psalms 141:3; Jeremiah 3:19; Exodus 33:4; Psalms 21:10; Psalms 83:12; Psalms 83:14; Psalms 84:7; Psalms 88:9; Psalms 110:1; Isaiah 5:6; Isaiah 16:3; Jeremiah 2:15; Jeremiah 13:16; Jeremiah 22:6; Jeremiah 50:3; Hosea 2:5; Genesis 4:25; Genesis 3:15; Exodus 10:1; Exodus 23:31; Job 38:36; Psalms 9:21; Psalms 21:7; Psalms 104:20; Proverbs 26:24; Isaiah 15:9; Jeremiah 51:39; Hosea 6:11; Exodus 21:22; Exodus 21:30; Numbers 12:11; Genesis 41:33; 2 Samuel 22:12; 1 Kings 11:34; Psalms 8:7; Psalms 18:12; Psalms 45:17; Isaiah 26:1; Job 14:13; Job 9:33; Psalms 139:5; Exodus 23:1; Exodus 7:23; 1 Samuel 4:20; 2 Samuel 13:20; Job 7:17; Psalms 48:14; Psalms 62:11; Proverbs 22:17; Proverbs 24:32; Proverbs 27:23; Jeremiah 31:21; Psalms 90:8; Psalms 101:3; Psalms 73:28; Psalms 17:11; Psalms 13:3; Psalms 73:9; Numbers 24:1; Psalms 21:13


## שֵׂךְ (id: 007034000000000)

שֵׂךְ
=====

שֵׂךְ (H7899)

noun m · שׂכך

Plants

thorn, prick, barb \= a stiff, sharp\-pointed woody projection on the stem or other part of a plant; ≈ associated with things that cause a constant, though not life\-threatening, irritation

[NUM 33:55](https://ref.ly/Num33:55)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 33:55


## שֹׂךְ (id: 007035000000000)

שֹׂךְ
=====

שֹׂךְ (H7900)

noun m · סכך

Tents

shelter, booth, hut \= a shelter, ◄ made of fragile materials such as leaves and branches, ► for the purpose of protection from rain and sun for both humans and animals; used by people who needed a temporary shelter, such as nomads, soldiers, and farm workers in the field; ≈ by extension, a metaphor for Jerusalem, the place where God dwells

[LAM 2:6](https://ref.ly/Lam2:6)

Anne G. Kompaoré

* **Associated Passages:** Lamentations 2:6


## שׁכב (id: 007345000000000)

שׁכב
====

שׁכב (H7901)

verb · מִשְׁכָּב · שְׁכָבָה · שְׁכֹבֶת

Stance \| verb qal \| ≈ כרע · נפל \| ≠ נשׂא · קום

to lie, to lie down \= state or action by which a human being or animal is in or assumes a horizontal position; ► in order to rest, sleep, or have sex with someone, or because of being unable to stand up as a result of weakness or death; ≈ sometimes associated with security

Stance \| verb hif, verb hof

to lay down, to make lie down, to tilt, to tip over \= causative action by which a human being or deity causes an object or human being to assume a horizontal position

Dead \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁכב עִם־אֲבֹות

to lie down with one's fathers \> to rest with one's ancestors literally: to lie down with one's fathers; hence: \= process by which a human being dies and so shares the fate and destination of his/her ancestors; ≈ only said of people in positions demanding respect, such as patriarchs and kings

Space

to lie \> to be (in a location, without defense) literally: to lie in a certain place; hence: \= state in which humans are located in a certain place, with the connotation of being in a vulnerable position

[PSA 57:5](https://ref.ly/Ps57:5)

End

to rest \> to stop, to give up, to have respite literally: to rest; hence: \= state in which an event stops taking place, as if in need of rest

Bear \| verb qal \| ≈ כסה

to lie down in \> to accept, to bear literally: to lie down in a certain state; hence: \= acts by which a human being accepts and acquiesces in a certain state

[JER 3:25](https://ref.ly/Jer3:25)

## שִׁכְבָּה (id: 007346000000000)

שִׁכְבָּה
=========

שִׁכְבָּה (H7902) — שִׁכְבָה, שְׁכָבָה

noun f · שׁכב

Cover

**Collocation:** שִׁכְבַת הַטָּל

layer of dew \= a layer of dew covering the ground

[EXO 16:13](https://ref.ly/Exod16:13) · [EXO 16:14](https://ref.ly/Exod16:14)

Body Products

**Collocation:** שִׁכְבַת זֶרַע

discharge of semen, emission of semen \= quantity of semen ◄ emitted by the human body spontaneously or during sexual intercourse

[LEV 15:16](https://ref.ly/Lev15:16) · [LEV 15:17](https://ref.ly/Lev15:17) · [LEV 15:18](https://ref.ly/Lev15:18) · [LEV 15:32](https://ref.ly/Lev15:32) · [LEV 19:20](https://ref.ly/Lev19:20) · [LEV 22:4](https://ref.ly/Lev22:4)

Copulate \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁכב שִׁכְבַת זֶרַע

to have sex literally: to lie with someone the lying of seed; hence: \= action by which humans have sexual intercourse with someone else, normally resulting in an emission of semen

[NUM 5:13](https://ref.ly/Num5:13)

Reinier de Blois

**Collocation:** שׂים שֶׂכֶל

Turn \| verb hif

**Collocation:** פנה שְׁכֶם

to turn (one's) back literally: to turn (one's) shoulder; hence: \= action by which humans turn around in order to move away from the place they were facing earlier

Turn \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁית שְׁכֵם

[PSA 21:13](https://ref.ly/Ps21:13)

Willing

**Collocation:** שְׁכֶם אֶחָד

with one accord literally: one shoulder; hence: \= state in which a humans are willing to work together towards a certain goal

[ZEP 3:9](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:9)

Dwell \| verb pi, verb hif

to cause to dwell \= causative action by which deities cause humans to make a certain place or area their home

[NUM 14:30](https://ref.ly/Num14:30) · [PSA 78:55](https://ref.ly/Ps78:55) · [JER 7:3](https://ref.ly/Jer7:3) · [JER 7:7](https://ref.ly/Jer7:7)

Dwell \| verb pi, verb pa

**Collocation:** שׁכן שֵׁם

to cause (one's) name to dwell, to establish (one's) name literally: to cause (one's) name to dwell (somewhere); hence: \= action by which deities accept a certain place as their abode and make it bear their name

[DEU 12:11](https://ref.ly/Deut12:11) · [DEU 14:23](https://ref.ly/Deut14:23) · [DEU 16:2](https://ref.ly/Deut16:2) · [DEU 16:6](https://ref.ly/Deut16:6) · [DEU 16:11](https://ref.ly/Deut16:11) · [DEU 26:2](https://ref.ly/Deut26:2) · [EZR 6:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:12) · [NEH 1:9](https://ref.ly/Neh1:9) · [JER 7:12](https://ref.ly/Jer7:12)

Dead \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁכן דּוּמָה

to dwell in silence \> to go down to the world of the dead literally: to dwell in silence; hence: \= process by which humans die and go down to the world of the dead

[PSA 94:17](https://ref.ly/Ps94:17)

Dead \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁכן כָּבֹוד לֶעָפָר

to make (someone) dwell in the dust literally: to cause (one's) glory (or one's self) to dwell towards the dust; hence: \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to die a dishonorable death and go down to the world of the dead

[PSA 7:6](https://ref.ly/Ps7:6)

Dead \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁכן עָפָר

to dwell in the dust literally: to live in the dust; hence: \= state in which humans have died and are in the world of the dead

[ISA 26:19](https://ref.ly/Isa26:19)

Engage \| verb hif

to let dwell, to let stay, to let abide, to let reside literally: (a quality) dwells; hence: \= causative process by which humans allow a certain quality to become integrated into their way of life

[JOB 11:14](https://ref.ly/Job11:14)

Space \| verb hif

to cause to settle, to place, to set up literally: to cause to dwell; hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities cause objects or living creatures to be located in a certain place for an extended period of time

[GEN 3:24](https://ref.ly/Gen3:24) · [JOS 18:1](https://ref.ly/Josh18:1) · [EZK 32:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:4)

Stance \| verb qal \| ≈ נום

to lie down exact meaning unsure; literally: to dwell; hence, probably: \= action by which humans lie down and refrain from any activity

[NAM 3:18](https://ref.ly/Nah3:18)

Weak \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁכן בָּתֵּי חֹמֶר

to dwell in houses of clay \> to be a mortal human being literally: to dwell in houses of clay; hence: \= state of being a mortal human being, living in a body that is bound to return to dust one day

[JOB 4:19](https://ref.ly/Job4:19)

=======

שָׁלֹום (H7965)

noun m · שׁלם · אַבְשָׁלֹום · שְׁלֹמֹה

Complete

completely, wholly \= state in which an entire group of people undergoes a certain event, without exceptions

[JER 13:19](https://ref.ly/Jer13:19)

Safe \| ≈ בֶּטַח · טֹוב · טֹובָה · מִבְטָח · מַרְפֵּא · שַׁאֲנָן · שׁלה · שַׁלְוָה · שׁקט · שֶׁקֶט \| ≠ חֶרֶב · חֲרָדָה · מִלְחָמָה · פַּחַד · רָע · רָעָב · רָעָה

peace, harmony, well\-being, welfare, prosperity, safety literally: completeness; hence: \= condition in which there is no conflict, war, danger, sickness, famine, or anything to fear, but where one can live at ease and with confidence

Move \| verb pi, verb qal, verb pe'al, verb pu'al

to send (messengers through an area), to send (messengers among people) \= causative action by which humans or deities commission people to travel through a certain area or among a certain nation in order to perform a certain task

[JDG 15:5](https://ref.ly/Judg15:5) · [1SA 31:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:9) · [1CH 10:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:9) · [2CH 24:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:19) · [JER 49:14](https://ref.ly/Jer49:14) · [DAN 3:2](https://ref.ly/Dan3:2) · [OBA 1:1](https://ref.ly/Obad1:1)

Move \| verb pi, verb hif, verb pu'al

to send (messengers) against (someone) \= causative action by which deities commission one or more people to go to a certain individual or place to inflict punishment

Move \| verb qal, verb pi, verb pe'al

to send word (to someone) \= causative action by which humans or deities send one or more people \-\- though the people sent are not mentioned explicitly \-\- to a certain individual or place in order to perform a certain task

Move \| verb qal

Move \| verb qal, verb nif, verb pi

Move \| verb qal, verb pi, verb pu'al

Move \| verb pi

to let down \= causative action by which humans allow an object to sink slowly into a place situated below oneself, usually with the help of ropes

Move \| verb pi

to cause to run \= causative action by which deities cause water to move from a certain location

[PSA 104:10](https://ref.ly/Ps104:10)

Birth \| verb pi \| ≈ פלח

to give birth literally: to send out (one's young); hence: \= process by which animals give birth

Burn \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁלח בָּאֵשׁ

to set fire literally: to send in the fire; hence: \= causative action by which humans set fire to an object

Non\-Whole \| verb pi

to cast off, to throw off \= causative action by which humans remove an object that is attached to part of their body

[JOB 30:11](https://ref.ly/Job30:11)

Free \| verb pi, verb qal

to release, to let go, to let loose, to set free \= causative action by which humans allow (other) humans or animals to move away freely from a place where they have been confined

Speak \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁלח פֶּה

to give (one's) mouth free rein literally: to release one's mouth; hence: \= causative action by which humans allow their mouth to utter certain words

Happen \| verb pi, verb qal, verb hif \| ≈ בוא

to send, to cause to happen, to stir up literally: to send; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause an event to happen

Stage \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁלח דָּלִית · שׁלח פֹּארָה · שׁלח קָצִיר · שׁלח שֹׁרֶשׁ

to send out, to extend literally: to send out one's branches or roots; hence: \= causative action by which plants cause their branches or roots to grow and extend in a certain direction

Stage \| verb pi

to let one's hair grow long literally: to send out one's hair; hence: \= causative action by which humans allow their hair to grow long

to stretch out, to extend, to wield \= action by which humans stretch out their hand, which holds an object, in order to do something with that object

Stance \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁלח רַגְלַיִם

to thrust aside (someone's) feet \> to send (someone) sprawling literally: to send away one's feet; hence, possible: \= causative action by which humans thrust aside the feet of someone who is standing up ► in order to make that person fall down; ≈ seen as an act of aggression

[JOB 30:12](https://ref.ly/Job30:12)5 · [ISA 32:20](https://ref.ly/Isa32:20)

Unwilling \| verb pi, verb pu'al

to deliver, to give over, to forsake \= action by which humans or deities stop caring for (other) humans or objects as if that person or object has been sent away from their presence

[JOB 8:4](https://ref.ly/Job8:4) · [PSA 81:13](https://ref.ly/Ps81:13) · [PRO 29:15](https://ref.ly/Prov29:15) · [ISA 27:10](https://ref.ly/Isa27:10)

1 2CH 17:7 Probably an aramaism 2 JER 27:3 · PSA 57:4 read וְשִׁלַּחְתָּם as וְשִׁלַּחְתָּ with RSV NIV NRSV REB GNT 3 EZK 31:5 MT HOTTP\-B NIV ("spreading") NJPS ("that welled up for it") NJB ("that made it grow") \| RSV NRSV REB "shoots, channels" \= שֶׁלַח 4 2SA 22:17 · PSA 18:17 The word יָד is not in the text but clearly implied 5 JOB 30:12 MT HOTTP\-B RSV ("they drive me forth") NIV ("they lay snares for my feet") NJPS ("they put me to flight") NRSV ("they send me sprawling") GNT ("they send me running) \| NJB וירגלו שׁלוי "to see when I am having a little peace" \= רגל and שָׁלֵו \| REB omit

## שׁלט (id: 007398000000000)

שׁלט
====

שׁלט (H7980 · A7981)

verb · שִׁלְטֹון · שַׁלֶּטֶת · שַׁלִּיט · שִׁלְטֹן · שָׁלְטָן

Control \| verb qal, verb pe'al

to control, to rule, to be in charge, to have power over \= process by which humans gain control over other humans or objects

[EST 9:1](https://ref.ly/Esth9:1) · [EST 9:1](https://ref.ly/Esth9:1) · [ECC 2:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:19) · [DAN 2:39](https://ref.ly/Dan2:39) · [DAN 3:27](https://ref.ly/Dan3:27) · [DAN 5:7](https://ref.ly/Dan5:7) · [DAN 5:16](https://ref.ly/Dan5:16)

Control \| verb qal, verb pe'al

to lord it over, to overpower \= action by which humans or wild animals exercise control over (other) humans, ► and so have the ability to do with them as they please; ≈ mostly used in a negative sense

[NEH 5:15](https://ref.ly/Neh5:15) · [ECC 8:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:9) · [DAN 6:25](https://ref.ly/Dan6:25)

Control \| verb hif

to give control \= causative action by which deities give humans power over objects, ► and so enable that them to handle these objects as they please

[ECC 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:18) · [ECC 6:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:2) · [DAN 2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:38) · [DAN 2:48](https://ref.ly/Dan2:48)

Control \| verb hif

to give control \= causative action by which deities give a certain condition control over a human being ► and so enable it to influence that person's behavior

[PSA 119:133](https://ref.ly/Ps119:133)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Esther 9:1; Ecclesiastes 2:19; Daniel 2:39; Daniel 3:27; Daniel 5:7; Daniel 5:16; Nehemiah 5:15; Ecclesiastes 8:9; Daniel 6:25; Ecclesiastes 5:18; Ecclesiastes 6:2; Daniel 2:38; Daniel 2:48; Psalms 119:133

## שָׁלָךְ (id: 007410000000000)

שָׁלָךְ
=======

שָׁלָךְ (H7994)

noun m

Birds

osprey \= an eagle\-like bird that feeds on fish. It has a white head, chest, and belly, with a dark brown back and wings. It has a dark stripe from the edge of the beak through the eye to the back of the neck and a faint stripe on its chest; ◄ Pandion haliaetus; it is found on seacoasts and near lakes and large rivers; ► unclean and therefore not to be eaten

[LEV 11:17](https://ref.ly/Lev11:17)1 · [DEU 14:17](https://ref.ly/Deut14:17)1

1 LEV 11:17 · DEU 14:17 RSV NIV NJPS NJB NRSV GNT CEV ESV "cormorant" \| REB "fisher\-owl"

Sarah Lind

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 11:17; Deuteronomy 14:17


## שׁלך (id: 007409000000000)

שׁלך
====

שׁלך (H7993)

verb · שַׁלֶּכֶת

Move \| verb hif, verb hof

to cast, to throw, to hurl \= causative action by which animals, deities, or humans cause an object to move through the air with some speed in a certain direction; ≈ often associated with some degree of rejection

11:13](https://ref.ly/Zech11:13)

Move \| verb hif, verb hof

to hurl out, to cast away literally: to cast away; hence: \= causative process by which humans leave their country because they are forced to settle somewhere else for an indefinite amount of time; ≈ associated with rejection

[DEU 29:27](https://ref.ly/Deut29:27) · [JER 22:28](https://ref.ly/Jer22:28) · [AMO 4:3](https://ref.ly/Amos4:3)

Blow \| verb hif

to hurl oneself (at someone) literally: (wind) hurls (itself at someone); hence: \= process by which the wind blows with strength and causes strong discomfort to people

[JOB 27:22](https://ref.ly/Job27:22)

Bear \| verb hif, verb hof

to cast (someone on someone else), to entrust (someone) literally: to cast (someone or something on someone else); hence: \= causative action by which humans entrust other humans to the care of a person or situation

[PSA 22:11](https://ref.ly/Ps22:11) · [PSA 55:23](https://ref.ly/Ps55:23)

Non\-Bear \| verb hif

to shake off literally: to cast away (one's actions); hence: \= action by which humans do what is needed to no longer bear the responsibility for their actions

[EZK 18:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:31)

Detach \| verb hif \| ≈ חמס

to let go, to shed, to drop literally: to cast off; hence: \= process by which a plant lets go of its fruits

[JOB 15:33](https://ref.ly/Job15:33)

Free \| verb hif

to snatch (someone's victim from its control) literally: to cast, throw, hurl (something from somewhere); hence: causative action by which humans use force to cause other humans to let their victims go free, ◄ like someone forcing a wild animal to drop its prey which its carries in its mouth

[JOB 29:17](https://ref.ly/Job29:17)

Fail \| verb hif

(actions) overthrow (the perpetrator) literaly: (actions) throw (someone) down; hence: \= causative process by which certain actions cause humans to fail in their undertakings

[JOB 18:7](https://ref.ly/Job18:7)

End \| verb hif

to cast down (splendor, truth) literally: to cast down (a condition); hence: \= causative process by which circumstances or divine intervention bring a certain condition to a sudden end; ≈ associated with humiliation

[LAM 2:1](https://ref.ly/Lam2:1) · [DAN 8:12](https://ref.ly/Dan8:12)

Innocent \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁלך חֲטָאִים אַחֲרֵי גַּו

to cast behind (one's) back, to forgive literally: to cast (someone else's) sins behind (one's) back; \= action by which deities stop considering (someone's) sinful actions and treat them as something that never happened

[ISA 38:17](https://ref.ly/Isa38:17)

Impact \| verb hif, verb hof

to tear down literally: to cast down (a construction); hence: \= causative action by which humans destroy a building or other construction

[JER 9:18](https://ref.ly/Jer9:18) · [DAN 8:11](https://ref.ly/Dan8:11)

Unsafe \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁלך נֶפֶשׁ מִנֶּגֶד

to risk (one's) life literally, probably: to cast (one's) life away from (oneself); hence: \= action by which humans intentionally expose themselves to danger to achieve a certain goal

[JDG 9:17](https://ref.ly/Judg9:17)

Unwilling \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁלך · שׁלך מִלְּפָנֶה · שׁלך מֵעַל־פַּנֶה · שׁלך מִפָּנֶה

to cast from (one's) presence, to reject literally: to cast (someone) from (one's face); hence: \= action by which deities end their relationship with humans and refuse to have anything to do with them anymore

[2KI 13:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:23) · [2KI 17:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:20) · [2KI 24:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:20) · [2CH 7:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:20) · [PSA 51:13](https://ref.ly/Ps51:13) · [PSA 71:9](https://ref.ly/Ps71:9) · [JER 7:15](https://ref.ly/Jer7:15) · [JER 7:15](https://ref.ly/Jer7:15) · [JER 52:3](https://ref.ly/Jer52:3)

Unwilling \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁלך אַחַר · שׁלך אַחֲרֵי גַּו

to cast behind (one's) back, to reject literally: to cast (a deity) behind (one's) back; hence: \= action by which humans refuse to continue to worship a certain deity

[1KI 14:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:9) · [NEH 9:26](https://ref.ly/Neh9:26) · [PSA 50:17](https://ref.ly/Ps50:17) · [EZK 23:35](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:35)

1 ECC 3:5 The meaning of this collocation is unsure \| among the possibilities are: (1\) to throw stones on a field to make it unsuitable for cultivation, (2\) to throw stones that were found on a field aside, (3\) to lay bricks, (4\) a euphemism for having sex, etc.

Reinier de Blois

## שֶׁלֶם (id: 007417000000000)

שֶׁלֶם
======

שֶׁלֶם (H8002)

noun m · שׁלם

Sacrifice

offering of well\-being, peace\-offering \= action by which humans offer an animal without blemish to a deity, whereby the animal is slaughtered, its fat is burned on the altar, part of the meat is given to the officiating priest, and the rest of the meat is eaten by the person bringing the sacrifice; ◄ out of thankfulness, as a freewill offering, or when fulfilling a vow; ≈ associated with joy and well\-being

## שָׁלֵם (id: 007418000000000)

שָׁלֵם
======

שָׁלֵם (H8003 · H8004)

adj · שׁלם

Whole

whole, complete, intact, full \= state in which an object or group of objects is in its full state, without anything added to it or removed from it

[DEU 25:15](https://ref.ly/Deut25:15) · [DEU 25:15](https://ref.ly/Deut25:15) · [DEU 27:6](https://ref.ly/Deut27:6) · [JOS 8:31](https://ref.ly/Josh8:31) · [1KI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:7) · [2CH 8:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:16) · [PRO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Prov11:1) · [AMO 1:6](https://ref.ly/Amos1:6) · [AMO 1:9](https://ref.ly/Amos1:9) · [NAM 1:12](https://ref.ly/Nah1:12)1

Complete

complete, finished, full literally: complete, intact; hence: \= state in which a process or action is fully completed

[GEN 15:16](https://ref.ly/Gen15:16) · [RUT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:12)

Faithful \| ≈ אֱמוּנָה · אֱמֶת

**Collocation:** לֵב שָׁלֵם · לֵבָב שָׁלֵם

wholehearted, sincere literally: (one's) heart is complete; hence: \= state in which humans are fully committed to a deity or a certain activity

[1KI 8:61](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:61) · [1KI 11:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:4) · [1KI 15:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:3) · [1KI 15:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:14) · [2KI 20:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:3) · [1CH 12:39](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:39) · [1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9) · [1CH 29:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:9) · [1CH 29:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:19) · [2CH 15:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:17) · [2CH 16:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:9) · [2CH 19:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:9) · [2CH 25:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:2) · [ISA 38:3](https://ref.ly/Isa38:3)

Safe

safe literally: complete; hence: \= state in which humans are unharmed

[GEN 33:18](https://ref.ly/Gen33:18)

Friends

friend, ally literally: (person of) peace; hence: \= someone with whom one has a good relationship and from whom one would expect some degree of loyalty

[GEN 34:21](https://ref.ly/Gen34:21)

name

Names of Locations

Salem \= town, to be identified with Jerusalem; ► ruled by Melchizedek; time of Abraham

[GEN 14:18](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18) · [PSA 76:3](https://ref.ly/Ps76:3)
