## שׁלם (id: 007414000000000)

שׁלם
====

שׁלם (H7999 · A8000)

verb · שָׁלֹום · שִׁלּוּם · שֶׁלֶם · שָׁלֵם · שִׁלֵּם · שִׁלֻּמָה · שַׁלְמֹנִים · שְׁלָם · שַׁלּוּם · שִׁלֵּם

Whole \| verb qal, verb pe'al

to be finished \= process by which a construction is finished

[EZR 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:16) · [NEH 6:15](https://ref.ly/Neh6:15)

Whole \| verb pi

to finish \= causative process by which humans cause a construction to be finished

[1KI 9:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:25)

Complete \| verb qal

to be completed, to be finished literally: to finish; hence: \= process by which a projects comes to completion

[1KI 7:51](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:51) · [2CH 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:1) · [ISA 60:20](https://ref.ly/Isa60:20)

Complete \| verb pi, verb pu'al, verb hif

to complete, to fulfill literally: to complete (a vow, prediction, or task); hence: \= action by which humans bring an obligation to an end by fulfilling its terms or carrying out its stipulations

[DEU 23:22](https://ref.ly/Deut23:22) · [2SA 15:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:7) · [JOB 22:27](https://ref.ly/Job22:27) · [JOB 23:14](https://ref.ly/Job23:14) · [PSA 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ps22:26) · [PSA 50:14](https://ref.ly/Ps50:14) · [PSA 56:13](https://ref.ly/Ps56:13) · [PSA 61:9](https://ref.ly/Ps61:9) · [PSA 65:2](https://ref.ly/Ps65:2) · [PSA 66:13](https://ref.ly/Ps66:13) · [PSA 76:12](https://ref.ly/Ps76:12) · [PSA 116:14](https://ref.ly/Ps116:14) · [PSA 116:18](https://ref.ly/Ps116:18) · [PRO 7:14](https://ref.ly/Prov7:14) · [ECC 5:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:3) · [ECC 5:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:3) · [ECC 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:4) · [ISA 19:21](https://ref.ly/Isa19:21) · [ISA 44:26](https://ref.ly/Isa44:26) · [ISA 44:28](https://ref.ly/Isa44:28) · [HOS 14:3](https://ref.ly/Hos14:3)1 · [JON 2:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:10) · [NAM 2:1](https://ref.ly/Nah2:1)

Dead \| verb hif

to bring (someone) to an end literally: to complete someone; hence, probably: \= causative action by which a deity causes the life of a human being to come to completion

[ISA 38:12](https://ref.ly/Isa38:12)2 · [ISA 38:13](https://ref.ly/Isa38:13)2

End \| verb haf'el

to bring to an end literally: to complete (an event); hence: \= causative action by which deities cause a situation to come to an end

[DAN 5:26](https://ref.ly/Dan5:26)

Non\-Possess \| verb pi, verb haf'el

to pay, to give back, to make restitution literally: to complete (what is owed); hence: \= causative action by which humans restore what was entrusted to them or substitute it with something else in case of loss or damage

[EXO 21:34](https://ref.ly/Exod21:34) · [EXO 21:36](https://ref.ly/Exod21:36) · [EXO 21:36](https://ref.ly/Exod21:36) · [EXO 21:37](https://ref.ly/Exod21:37) · [EXO 22:2](https://ref.ly/Exod22:2) · [EXO 22:2](https://ref.ly/Exod22:2) · [EXO 22:3](https://ref.ly/Exod22:3) · [EXO 22:4](https://ref.ly/Exod22:4) · [EXO 22:5](https://ref.ly/Exod22:5) · [EXO 22:5](https://ref.ly/Exod22:5) · [EXO 22:6](https://ref.ly/Exod22:6) · [EXO 22:8](https://ref.ly/Exod22:8) · [EXO 22:10](https://ref.ly/Exod22:10) · [EXO 22:11](https://ref.ly/Exod22:11) · [EXO 22:12](https://ref.ly/Exod22:12) · [EXO 22:13](https://ref.ly/Exod22:13) · [EXO 22:13](https://ref.ly/Exod22:13) · [EXO 22:14](https://ref.ly/Exod22:14) · [LEV 5:16](https://ref.ly/Lev5:16) · [LEV 5:24](https://ref.ly/Lev5:24) · [LEV 24:18](https://ref.ly/Lev24:18) · [LEV 24:21](https://ref.ly/Lev24:21) · [2SA 12:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:6) · [2KI 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:7) · [EZR 7:19](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:19) · [JOB 8:6](https://ref.ly/Job8:6) · [JOB 41:3](https://ref.ly/Job41:3) · [PSA 37:21](https://ref.ly/Ps37:21) · [PRO 6:31](https://ref.ly/Prov6:31) · [PRO 22:27](https://ref.ly/Prov22:27) · [EZK 33:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:15)

Chastise \| verb pi

to pay back literally: to pay back; hence: \= causative process by which deities or humans cause (other) humans to receive the appropriate return for their evil actions

[DEU 32:41](https://ref.ly/Deut32:41) · [2KI 9:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:26) · [JOB 21:19](https://ref.ly/Job21:19) · [JOB 34:33](https://ref.ly/Job34:33) · [PSA 31:24](https://ref.ly/Ps31:24) · [PSA 41:11](https://ref.ly/Ps41:11) · [PRO 20:22](https://ref.ly/Prov20:22) · [ISA 65:6](https://ref.ly/Isa65:6) · [JER 51:24](https://ref.ly/Jer51:24) · [JER 51:56](https://ref.ly/Jer51:56) · [JER 51:56](https://ref.ly/Jer51:56)

Chastise \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁלם אֶל־חֵיק · שׁלם עַל־חֵיק

to pay back literally: to pay back to (someone's) bosom; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to receive the appropriate return for their evil actions

[ISA 65:6](https://ref.ly/Isa65:6) · [JER 32:18](https://ref.ly/Jer32:18)

Chastise \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁלם אֶל־פַּנִים

to pay back literally: to pay back to (someone's) face; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to receive the appropriate return for their evil actions

[DEU 7:10](https://ref.ly/Deut7:10) · [DEU 7:10](https://ref.ly/Deut7:10)

Happen \| verb pi, verb pu'al

to reward, to repay, to render literally: to pay back; hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to be compensated for their actions, whether good or evil

Safe \| verb hof

to be caused to be at peace literally: to be made complete; hence: \= causative process by which wild animals are made to live in peace with humans

[JOB 5:23](https://ref.ly/Job5:23)

Safe \| verb hif

to make peace literally: to make complete; hence: \= action by which humans do what is necessary to be able to live without conflict with other humans

[DEU 20:12](https://ref.ly/Deut20:12) · [JOS 10:1](https://ref.ly/Josh10:1) · [JOS 10:4](https://ref.ly/Josh10:4) · [JOS 11:19](https://ref.ly/Josh11:19) · [2SA 10:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:19) · [1KI 22:45](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:45) · [1CH 19:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:19)

Safe \| verb hif

to cause to make peace, to reconcile literally: to make complete; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to do what is necessary to be able to live without conflict with other humans

[PRO 16:7](https://ref.ly/Prov16:7)

Friends \| verb qal ptc.act

friend literally: (who is of) peace; hence: \= someone with whom one has a good relationship and to whom one owes some degree of loyalty; ≈ only used in contexts where that loyalty is unexpectedly lacking

[PSA 7:5](https://ref.ly/Ps7:5)

Patient \| verb qal, verb pu'al

to be peaceable, to be at peace literally: to be peaceable; hence: \= state in which humans are inclined to avoid getting involved in conflicts

[2SA 20:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:19) · [JOB 22:21](https://ref.ly/Job22:21) · [ISA 42:19](https://ref.ly/Isa42:19)3

Strong \| verb qal

to remain unscathed \= process by which humans go through difficult circumstances without being hurt

[JOB 9:4](https://ref.ly/Job9:4)

1 HOS 14:3 reading פְּרִי "fruit" instead of פָּרִים "bulls" with LXX RSV NIV NRSV ESV GNT \| NJPS CEV "bulls" 2 ISA 38:12 · ISA 38:13 RSV NRSV ESV "bring me to an end" NIV "made an end of me" NJB "have been making an end of me" GNT "was ending my life" CEV "make a wreck of me" \| NJPS "was I kept whole" \| REB "you torment me" 3 ISA 42:19 RSV NRSV ESV "my dedicated one" NIV "the one committed to me" NJPS "the chosen one" REB "the one who has my trust" CEV "his chosen servant" \| NJB "friend" \= \[1o] \| LXX οἱ κυριεύοντες αὐτῶν "who lord it over them" \= משׁל

Reinier de Blois

## שַׂלְמָא (id: 007050000000000)

שַׂלְמָא
========

שַׂלְמָא (H8007)

name

Names of People \| ≈ שַׂלְמָה · שַׂלְמֹון

Salma, Salmah, Salmon \= man, also known as Salmah and Salmon; ◄ son of Nahshon; tribe of Judah; ► father of Boaz and ancestor of David

[1CH 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:11) · [1CH 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:11)

Names of People

Salma \= man; ◄ descendant of Caleb; tribe of Judah; ► founder of Bethlehem

[1CH 2:51](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:51) · [1CH 2:54](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:54)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 2:11; 1 Chronicles 2:51; 1 Chronicles 2:54

שְׁלֹמֹה (H8010)

name

Solomon \= man; ◄ son of David; tribe of Judah; ► king of Judah and Israel; given the name Jedidiah at his birth

## שַׂלְמָה (id: 007051000000000)

שַׂלְמָה
========

שַׂלְמָה (H8008 · H8009)

noun f · שִׂמְלָה

Clothing

mantle, cloak \= a long, flowing outer garment reaching to the feet; it was generally a loose\-fitting cloth that was draped around the body and held in place in the middle by a sash wrapped around the waist or some means of fastening such as pins or buttons; it was worn at all levels of society, including also priests and other religious leaders; it served more purposes than just an outer covering; women sometimes carried bowls or other implements or grain in its folds; for poorer people it also served as a covering when sleeping

[EXO 22:8](https://ref.ly/Exod22:8) · [EXO 22:25](https://ref.ly/Exod22:25) · [DEU 24:13](https://ref.ly/Deut24:13) · [DEU 29:4](https://ref.ly/Deut29:4) · [JOS 9:5](https://ref.ly/Josh9:5) · [JOS 9:13](https://ref.ly/Josh9:13) · [JOS 22:8](https://ref.ly/Josh22:8) · [1KI 10:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:25) · [1KI 11:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:29) · [1KI 11:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:30) · [2CH 9:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:24) · [NEH 9:21](https://ref.ly/Neh9:21) · [JOB 9:31](https://ref.ly/Job9:31) · [PSA 104:2](https://ref.ly/Ps104:2) · [SNG 4:11](https://ref.ly/Song4:11) · [MIC 2:8](https://ref.ly/Mic2:8)

name

Names of People \| ≈ שַׂלְמָא · שַׂלְמֹון

Salmah, Salma, Salmon \= man, also known as Salma and Salmon; ◄ son of Nahshon; tribe of Judah; ► father of Boaz and ancestor of David

[RUT 4:20](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:20)

Reinier de Blois

## שׁלשׁ (id: 007435000000000)

שׁלשׁ
=====

שׁלשׁ (H8027)

verb · שָׁלֹשׁ

Quantity \| verb pu'al ptc.pass

threefold, in three parts \= state in which an object consists of three elements or parts

[ECC 4:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:12) · [EZK 42:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek42:6)

Non\-Whole \| verb pi

to divide into three parts \= causative action by which humans divide an object into three parts

[DEU 19:3](https://ref.ly/Deut19:3)

Frequency \| verb pi

to do for the third time \= action by which humans repeat an activity for the third time

[1KI 18:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:34) · [1KI 18:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:34)

Stage \| verb pu'al ptc.pass

three years old \= state in which an animal is three years old

[GEN 15:9](https://ref.ly/Gen15:9) · [GEN 15:9](https://ref.ly/Gen15:9) · [GEN 15:9](https://ref.ly/Gen15:9)

Time \| verb pi

to count three days \= action by which humans count three days from the time in focus, which is the day after the next day

[1SA 20:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:19)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Ecclesiastes 4:12; Ezekiel 42:6; Deuteronomy 19:3; 1 Kings 18:34; Genesis 15:9; 1 Samuel 20:19

## שִׁלְשֹׁום (id: 007440000000000)

שִׁלְשֹׁום
==========

שִׁלְשֹׁום (H8032)

noun m · שָׁלֹשׁ · יֹום

Temporal Referents

**Collocation:** תְּמֹול שִׁלְשֹׁום

(what used to be done) before, in the past \= reference to times in the past when a certain activity used to take place repeatedly

3:15](https://ref.ly/Nah3:15) · [HAB 3:4](https://ref.ly/Hab3:4) · [HAG 2:14](https://ref.ly/Hag2:14) · [ZEC 5:11](https://ref.ly/Zech5:11)

Spatial Referents

here ... there ... literally: there ... there ...; hence: \= a reference to a variety of locations

[ISA 28:10](https://ref.ly/Isa28:10) · [ISA 28:10](https://ref.ly/Isa28:10) · [ISA 28:13](https://ref.ly/Isa28:13) · [ISA 28:13](https://ref.ly/Isa28:13)

Exist

there is literally: there (but without a specific location to refer to); hence: \= marker identifying a certain event as real

[ECC 3:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:17)

Identifiers

behold! literally: there (but without a specifc location to refer to); hence: \= marker drawing the reader's attention to an event

[PSA 14:5](https://ref.ly/Ps14:5) · [PSA 36:13](https://ref.ly/Ps36:13) · [PSA 53:6](https://ref.ly/Ps53:6) · [PSA 68:28](https://ref.ly/Ps68:28)

Temporal Referents

then, at that time literally: there; hence: \= a reference to a point of time specified in the immediate context

[ZEP 1:14](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:14)

1 GEN 49:24 NJPS "there" ESV \| RSV NJB NRSV "name" \= שֵׁם \| LXX "δυνάστου" REB GNT "power" CEV "help" 2 HOS 6:7 This interpretation requires כְּאָדָם to be read as בְּאָדָם.

## שֻׁם (id: 007444000000000)

שֻׁם
====

שֻׁם (A8036)

noun m

Name

name \= one or more words by which a person, deity, place or object is known, addressed, remembered, or referred to; ≈ regarded as a core element of one's identity

[EZR 5:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:4) · [EZR 5:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:10) · [EZR 5:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:10) · [EZR 5:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:14) · [DAN 2:26](https://ref.ly/Dan2:26) · [DAN 4:5](https://ref.ly/Dan4:5) · [DAN 4:5](https://ref.ly/Dan4:5) · [DAN 4:16](https://ref.ly/Dan4:16) · [DAN 5:12](https://ref.ly/Dan5:12)

Control

name, authority literally: name; hence: \= state in which someone acts with the authority of someone else

[EZR 5:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:1)

Self

name, self literally: name; hence: \= the core of one's identity, which reflect the essence of one's being

[EZR 6:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:12) · [DAN 2:20](https://ref.ly/Dan2:20)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Ezra 5:4; Ezra 5:10; Ezra 5:14; Daniel 2:26; Daniel 4:5; Daniel 4:16; Daniel 5:12; Ezra 5:1; Ezra 6:12; Daniel 2:20

## שְׂמֹאל (id: 007055000000000)

שְׂמֹאל
=======

שְׂמֹאל (H8040) — שְׂמֹואל

noun m

Spatial Referents \| ≠ יָמִין

left side, left \= a referent pointing to the north side of the human body when facing the sunrise, ≈ in extension: one option as opposed to another

[GEN 13:9](https://ref.ly/Gen13:9) · [GEN 24:49](https://ref.ly/Gen24:49) · [GEN 48:13](https://ref.ly/Gen48:13) · [GEN 48:13](https://ref.ly/Gen48:13) · [GEN 48:14](https://ref.ly/Gen48:14) · [EXO 14:22](https://ref.ly/Exod14:22) · [EXO 14:29](https://ref.ly/Exod14:29) · [NUM 20:17](https://ref.ly/Num20:17) · [NUM 22:26](https://ref.ly/Num22:26) · [DEU 2:27](https://ref.ly/Deut2:27) · [DEU 5:32](https://ref.ly/Deut5:32) · [DEU 17:11](https://ref.ly/Deut17:11) · [DEU 17:20](https://ref.ly/Deut17:20) · [DEU 28:14](https://ref.ly/Deut28:14) · [JOS 1:7](https://ref.ly/Josh1:7) · [JOS 23:6](https://ref.ly/Josh23:6) · [JDG 3:21](https://ref.ly/Judg3:21) · [JDG 7:20](https://ref.ly/Judg7:20) · [JDG 16:29](https://ref.ly/Judg16:29) · [1SA 6:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:12) · [2SA 2:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:19) · [2SA 2:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:21) · [2SA 16:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:6) · [1KI 22:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:19) · [2KI 22:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:2) · [2KI 23:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:8) · [1CH 6:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:29) · [2CH 18:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:18) · [2CH 34:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:2) · [NEH 8:4](https://ref.ly/Neh8:4) · [PRO 3:16](https://ref.ly/Prov3:16) · [PRO 4:27](https://ref.ly/Prov4:27) · [ECC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:2) · [SNG 2:6](https://ref.ly/Song2:6) · [SNG 8:3](https://ref.ly/Song8:3) · [ISA 9:19](https://ref.ly/Isa9:19) · [ISA 54:3](https://ref.ly/Isa54:3) · [EZK 1:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:10) · [EZK 39:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:3) · [DAN 12:7](https://ref.ly/Dan12:7) · [JON 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:11) · [ZEC 4:3](https://ref.ly/Zech4:3) · [ZEC 4:11](https://ref.ly/Zech4:11) · [ZEC 12:6](https://ref.ly/Zech12:6)

\| ≈ צָפֹון \| ≠ יָמִין

north, northern, northerly, northward ‎literally: left; hence: \= a referent pointing to the space to the left of a person when facing the direction of the rising sun

[GEN 14:15](https://ref.ly/Gen14:15) · [JOS 19:27](https://ref.ly/Josh19:27) · [1KI 7:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:39) · [1KI 7:49](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:49) · [2CH 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:17) · [2CH 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:6) · [2CH 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:7) · [2CH 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:8) · [JOB 23:9](https://ref.ly/Job23:9) · [EZK 16:46](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:46)

=====

שׂמאל (H8041)

verb · שְׂמֹאל · שְׂמָאלִי

Move \| verb hif \| ≠ ימן

to go to the left \= action by which humans turn to the left, when walking or performing another event; ≈ in extension: to choose one option as opposed to another

[GEN 13:9](https://ref.ly/Gen13:9) · [2SA 14:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:19) · [ISA 30:21](https://ref.ly/Isa30:21) · [EZK 21:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:21)

Engage \| verb hif \| ≠ ימן

to do (something) with the left hand \= action by which humans perform an action with the left hand

[1CH 12:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:2)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 13:9; 2 Samuel 14:19; Isaiah 30:21; Ezekiel 21:21; 1 Chronicles 12:2

adj · שׂמאל

## שׁמד (id: 007450000000000)

שׁמד
====

שׁמד (H8045 · A8046)

verb · שֶׁמֶד

Non\-Exist \| verb hif, verb haf'el, verb nif \| ≈ אבד · הפך · הרג · חרם · כחד · כלה · כרת · מות · מחה · נכה · נפל \| ≠ פרח · שׁמר

to destroy, to exterminate, to wipe out, to demolish \= causative action by which humans, deities, or events completely remove humans, animals, or objects from existence; ◄ by (a usually unnatural) death, aggression, hardship, disaster, destruction, or direct divine intervention; ► often as punishment

Non\-Contain \| ≈ עֲרָבָה · צִיָּה · שׁמד

Grieve \| ≈ אָלָה · חֹרֶב · חָרְבָּה · חֶרְפָּה · מָשָׁל · קְלָלָה · שְׁמָמָה · שְׁנִינָה · שַׁעֲרוּרָה · שְׁרֵקָה

## שׁמט (id: 007459000000000)

שׁמט
====

שׁמט (H8058)

verb

Release \| verb qal

to slip \= process by which an animal fails to grip or make proper contact with a surface

[2SA 6:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:6) · [1CH 13:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:9)

Release \| verb qal, verb nif

to drop, to let fall \= actions by which humans let go of an object held in their hands, ► so that it will fall

[2KI 9:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:33) · [2KI 9:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:33) · [PSA 141:6](https://ref.ly/Ps141:6)

Non\-Possess \| verb qal, verb hif

to release, to relinquish \= action by which humans relinquish their rights to an object, either temporarily or permanently

[EXO 23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:11) · [DEU 15:2](https://ref.ly/Deut15:2) · [DEU 15:3](https://ref.ly/Deut15:3) · [JER 17:4](https://ref.ly/Jer17:4)

1 2KI 9:33 MT\-K שׁמטהו \| MT\-Q שִׁמְטוּהָ

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Samuel 6:6; 1 Chronicles 13:9; 2 Kings 9:33; Psalms 141:6; Exodus 23:11; Deuteronomy 15:2; Deuteronomy 15:3; Jeremiah 17:4

## שָׁמַיִם (id: 007464000000000)

שָׁמַיִם
========

שָׁמַיִם (H8064)

noun m

Regions Above the Earth \| ≈ אֶרֶץ · יָם · מַיִם · מָרֹום · עָב · שַׁחַק · תֵּבֵל · תְּהֹום

heaven, sky \= the area above the earth, the location of sun, moon, and stars, the realm of the birds and God's primary residence; seen as the source of dew, rain, hail, snow, thunder and lightning, and sometimes even food; associated with what is high and remote, created by God and bound to disappear one day

## שְׁמַיִן (id: 007465000000000)

שְׁמַיִן
========

שְׁמַיִן (A8065)

noun m

Regions Above the Earth

heaven, sky \= the area above the earth, the location of sun, moon, and stars, the realm of the birds and God's primary residence; seen as the source of dew, rain, hail, snow, thunder and lightning, and sometimes even food; associated with what is high and remote, created by God and bound to disappear one day

## שִׂמְלָה (id: 007061000000000)

שִׂמְלָה
========

שִׂמְלָה (H8071)

noun f · שַׂלְמָה

Clothing

mantle, cloak \= a long, flowing outer garment reaching to the feet; it was generally a loose\-fitting cloth that was draped around the body and held in place in the middle by a sash wrapped around the waist or some means of fastening such as pins or buttons; it was worn at all levels of society, including also priests and other religious leaders; it served more purposes than just an outer covering; women sometimes carried bowls or other implements or grain in its folds; for poorer people it also served as a covering when sleeping

שְׂמָמִית (H8079)

noun f

Small Animals

gecko \= small type of lizard about 10 centimeters (4 inches) long with pads at the end of its toes with small hairs, enabling it to walk upside down on ceilings and overhangs; only type of lizard that produces sound; ◄ Hemidactylus turcicus, or a general word for all kinds of gecko; some varieties of geckos are often found inside buildings; ► feeds on insects; unclean animal and therefore not to be eaten

verb · אַשְׁמַנִּים · מִשְׁמָן · מַשְׁמַנִּים · שָׁמָן · שָׁמֵן · שֶׁמֶן

Fat \| verb qal, verb hif \| ≈ כשׂה · עבה · עשׁת

to be well\-fed literally: to be fat; hence: \= state in which humans or animals look healthy and strong ◄ as a result of an abundance of food; ≈ it is important to note that fatness is usually regarded as a good quality in the OT, even though the causes and consequences are not necessarily considered positive; sometimes, however, fatness is associated with dullness and unresponsiveness

[DEU 32:15](https://ref.ly/Deut32:15) · [DEU 32:15](https://ref.ly/Deut32:15) · [NEH 9:25](https://ref.ly/Neh9:25) · [JER 5:28](https://ref.ly/Jer5:28)

Foolish \| verb hif \| ≈ כבד · שׁעע

**Collocation:** שׁמן לֵב

to make (someone's) heart fat \> to make (someone's) mind dull and unresponsive literally: to cause (someone else's) heart to be fat; hence: \= causative action by which humans cause the mind of other humans to be dull and unresponsive

[ISA 6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:10)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 32:15; Nehemiah 9:25; Jeremiah 5:28; Isaiah 6:10

Hear \| verb hif \| ≈ עבר קֹול

to cause to hear, to proclaim, to announce \= causative process or action by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to perceive something, primarily \-\-though not necessarily exclusively\-\- with their ears, either as a spontaneous incident or as the result of focusing the ears on something

[DEU 4:10](https://ref.ly/Deut4:10) · [DEU 4:36](https://ref.ly/Deut4:36) · [DEU 30:12](https://ref.ly/Deut30:12) · [DEU 30:13](https://ref.ly/Deut30:13) · [JOS 6:10](https://ref.ly/Josh6:10) · [JDG 13:23](https://ref.ly/Judg13:23) · [JDG 18:25](https://ref.ly/Judg18:25) · [1SA 9:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:27) · [2KI 7:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:6) · [NEH 8:15](https://ref.ly/Neh8:15) · [PSA 26:7](https://ref.ly/Ps26:7) · [PSA 51:10](https://ref.ly/Ps51:10) · [PSA 66:8](https://ref.ly/Ps66:8) · [PSA 76:9](https://ref.ly/Ps76:9) · [PSA 106:2](https://ref.ly/Ps106:2) · [PSA 143:8](https://ref.ly/Ps143:8) · [SNG 2:14](https://ref.ly/Song2:14) · [SNG 8:13](https://ref.ly/Song8:13) · [ISA 30:30](https://ref.ly/Isa30:30) · [ISA 41:22](https://ref.ly/Isa41:22) · [ISA 41:26](https://ref.ly/Isa41:26) · [ISA 42:2](https://ref.ly/Isa42:2) · [ISA 42:9](https://ref.ly/Isa42:9) · [ISA 43:9](https://ref.ly/Isa43:9) · [ISA 43:12](https://ref.ly/Isa43:12) · [ISA 44:8](https://ref.ly/Isa44:8) · [ISA 45:21](https://ref.ly/Isa45:21) · [ISA 48:3](https://ref.ly/Isa48:3) · [ISA 48:5](https://ref.ly/Isa48:5) · [ISA 48:6](https://ref.ly/Isa48:6) · [ISA 48:20](https://ref.ly/Isa48:20) · [ISA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Isa52:7) · [ISA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Isa52:7) · [ISA 58:4](https://ref.ly/Isa58:4) · [ISA 62:11](https://ref.ly/Isa62:11) · [JER 4:5](https://ref.ly/Jer4:5) · [JER 4:15](https://ref.ly/Jer4:15) · [JER 4:16](https://ref.ly/Jer4:16) · [JER 5:20](https://ref.ly/Jer5:20) · [JER 18:2](https://ref.ly/Jer18:2) · [JER 23:22](https://ref.ly/Jer23:22) · [JER 31:7](https://ref.ly/Jer31:7) · [JER 46:14](https://ref.ly/Jer46:14) · [JER 46:14](https://ref.ly/Jer46:14) · [JER 48:4](https://ref.ly/Jer48:4) · [JER 49:2](https://ref.ly/Jer49:2) · [JER 50:2](https://ref.ly/Jer50:2) · [JER 50:2](https://ref.ly/Jer50:2) · [EZK 27:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:30) · [EZK 36:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:15) · [AMO 3:9](https://ref.ly/Amos3:9) · [AMO 4:5](https://ref.ly/Amos4:5) · [NAM 2:1](https://ref.ly/Nah2:1)

Gather \| verb hif, verb pi

to summon literally: to make (people) hear; hence: \= causative action by which humans let other humans know that they are to come

[1SA 15:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:4) · [1SA 23:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:8) · [1KI 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:22) · [JER 50:29](https://ref.ly/Jer50:29) · [JER 51:27](https://ref.ly/Jer51:27)

Judge \| verb qal

to hear a case \= action by which humans or deities investigate a dispute in order to be able to render a decision

[DEU 1:16](https://ref.ly/Deut1:16) · [DEU 1:17](https://ref.ly/Deut1:17) · [DEU 1:17](https://ref.ly/Deut1:17) · [JDG 11:10](https://ref.ly/Judg11:10)

Know \| verb nif

to be reported, to be proclaimed \= process by which information is passed on to individuals or among a larger group

[NEH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Neh6:1) · [NEH 6:6](https://ref.ly/Neh6:6) · [NEH 6:7](https://ref.ly/Neh6:7) · [EST 1:20](https://ref.ly/Esth1:20) · [EST 2:8](https://ref.ly/Esth2:8)

Know \| verb qal \| ≈ בִּינָה · ידע

to understand \= process by which humans are able to decipher the meaning of words spoken in another language

[GEN 11:7](https://ref.ly/Gen11:7) · [GEN 42:23](https://ref.ly/Gen42:23) · [DEU 28:49](https://ref.ly/Deut28:49) · [1KI 20:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:12) · [2KI 18:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:26) · [ISA 33:19](https://ref.ly/Isa33:19) · [ISA 36:11](https://ref.ly/Isa36:11) · [JER 5:15](https://ref.ly/Jer5:15) · [EZK 3:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:6)

Know \| verb qal

to discern \= state in which humans have the ability to understand an issue, process it, and draw the right conclusions

[2SA 14:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:17) · [1KI 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:9) · [1KI 3:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:11) · [NEH 8:2](https://ref.ly/Neh8:2)

Sing \| verb hif

to sing literally: to cause (one's voice) to be heard; \= action by which humans sing for a large audience

[2CH 5:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:13) · [NEH 12:42](https://ref.ly/Neh12:42)

Sounds \| verb hif \| ≈ קֹול · תְּרוּעָה

to sound literally: to cause (a musical instrument) to be heard; hence: \= action by which humans play a musical instrument for a large audience

[1CH 15:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:16) · [1CH 15:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:19) · [1CH 15:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:28) · [1CH 16:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:5) · [1CH 16:42](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:42)

Willing \| verb qal, verb nif, verb hitpa'al \| ≈ אבה · אזן · חלה · חפץ · חשׁב · נשׂא

to listen, to comply, to obey \= state in which humans or deities agree to a request or command and perform it

========

name · שׁמע

Names of People \| ≈ שִׁמְעָה · שִׁמְעִי · שַׁמָּה

Shimea, Shimeah, Shimei, Shammah \= man, also known as Shimeah, Shimei, and Shammah; ◄ third son of Jesse; brother of David; tribe of Judah

Names of People

Shimea \= man; ◄ son of David; tribe of Judah

[1CH 3:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr3:5)

Names of People

Shimea \= man; ◄ son of Uzzah; descendant of Merari; tribe of Levi

[1CH 6:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:15)

Names of People

Shimea \= man; ◄ son of Michael; tribe of Levi; ► ancestor of Asaph

[1CH 6:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:24)

Reinier de Blois

Know \| verb qal \| ≈ בין

to keep in mind, to remember \= action by which humans keep processing information in their minds

[GEN 37:11](https://ref.ly/Gen37:11) · [PSA 107:43](https://ref.ly/Ps107:43) · [PRO 4:21](https://ref.ly/Prov4:21)

Non\-Engage \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁמר יָד

to refrain (from an activity) literally: to keep one's hand; hence: \= action by which humans refrain from engaging in a certain activity

[ISA 56:2](https://ref.ly/Isa56:2)

Safe \| verb qal, verb nif, verb hit

to be careful, to make sure, to take precautions \= action by which humans ensure their own well\-being by being watchful, by making sure that certain instructions are carried out, or by refraining from certain activities

to watch over, to guard, to be in charge of, to take care of, to protect, to tend, to keep \= causative action by which humans or deities make sure that an object is safe from harm; ◄ by observing the object and the surrounding area carefully and intervening when necessary

## שָׁמְרָה (id: 007501000000000)

noun f · שׁמר

Non\-Speak

**Collocation:** שִׁית שָׁמרָה לְפֶה

to set a guard over (someone's) mouth literally: to place a guard over (someone's) mouth; hence: \= causative action by which deities help humans make sure that they will not say things that they should not say

Know

Know

**Collocation:** נֶגֶד שֶׁמֶשׁ

in broad daylight literally: opposite the sun; hence: \= state in which an event takes place in clear daylight, for everyone to see

east, eastern, easterly, eastward literally: the rising of the sun; hence: \= a referent pointing to the place where the sun rises

## שׁנה (id: 007527000000000)

שׁנה
====

שׁנה (A8133 · H8138) — שׁנא

verb · שָׁנָה · מִשְׁנֶה

Different \| verb qal, verb pe'al, verb pa

to be different, to change, to be affected \= process by which an object takes on different characteristics

[LAM 4:1](https://ref.ly/Lam4:1) · [DAN 3:27](https://ref.ly/Dan3:27) · [DAN 7:3](https://ref.ly/Dan7:3) · [DAN 7:7](https://ref.ly/Dan7:7) · [DAN 7:19](https://ref.ly/Dan7:19) · [MAL 3:6](https://ref.ly/Mal3:6)

Different \| verb qal

to be of various design, to be of different kinds \= state in which objects are not homogeneous in shape but differ from one another

[EST 1:7](https://ref.ly/Esth1:7)

Different \| verb qal

to be different, to dissent meaning unsure; literally: to be different; hence, possibly: \= state in which humans or deities differ from others in attitude, status, or opinion

[PRO 24:21](https://ref.ly/Prov24:21)1

Change \| verb qal, verb pe'al, verb etpa''al

to be changed, to be different \= process by which a condition takes on different characteristics

[EST 3:8](https://ref.ly/Esth3:8) · [DAN 2:9](https://ref.ly/Dan2:9) · [DAN 6:18](https://ref.ly/Dan6:18) · [DAN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Dan7:23) · [DAN 7:24](https://ref.ly/Dan7:24)

Change \| verb pi, verb pa, verb haf'el

to change (something), to alter \= causative process or action by which humans or deities cause a condition to take on different characteristics

[EZR 6:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:11) · [EZR 6:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:12) · [PSA 89:35](https://ref.ly/Ps89:35) · [PRO 31:5](https://ref.ly/Prov31:5) · [JER 2:36](https://ref.ly/Jer2:36) · [DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21) · [DAN 3:28](https://ref.ly/Dan3:28) · [DAN 4:13](https://ref.ly/Dan4:13) · [DAN 6:9](https://ref.ly/Dan6:9) · [DAN 6:16](https://ref.ly/Dan6:16) · [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25)

Change \| verb qal

**Collocation:** יָמִין שׁנה

(one's) saving power changes literally: (one's) right hand changes; hence, probably: \= process by which the frequency and/or intensity of deity's saving acts, associated with the right hand, change

[PSA 77:11](https://ref.ly/Ps77:11)2

Foolish \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁנה טַעַם

to act insane, to feign madness literally: to change one's good sense; hence: \= action by which humans act as if they lack good judgement

[1SA 21:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:14) · [PSA 34:1](https://ref.ly/Ps34:1)

Dress \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁנה בֶּגֶד

to change (clothes) literally: to change (one's) clothes; hence: \= action by which humans put on different clothes

[2KI 25:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:29) · [JER 52:33](https://ref.ly/Jer52:33)

Frequency \| verb nif

to happen twice \= process by which a another process happens twice

[GEN 41:32](https://ref.ly/Gen41:32)

Frequency \| verb qal

to repeat, to do (something) twice \= action by which humans perform an event twice

[1SA 26:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:8) · [2SA 20:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:10) · [1KI 18:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:34) · [1KI 18:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:34) · [NEH 13:21](https://ref.ly/Neh13:21) · [JOB 29:22](https://ref.ly/Job29:22) · [PRO 17:9](https://ref.ly/Prov17:9) · [PRO 26:11](https://ref.ly/Prov26:11)

Non\-Know \| verb hit

to disguise oneself literally: to change oneself; hence: \= action by which humans make themselves unrecognizable by others ◄ by changing their appearance or clothing

[1KI 14:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:2)

Respect \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁנה לְטֹוב

to treat (someone) better (than others) literally: to change (a person) for good; hence: \= action by which humans treat certain other humans better than others

[EST 2:9](https://ref.ly/Esth2:9)

Shape \| verb etpa''al

**Collocation:** צְלֵם אֲנַף שׁנה

(one's) expression is changed, (one's) face is distorted literally: the image of (someone's) face changes; hence: \= process by which the expression of someone's face is changed

[DAN 3:19](https://ref.ly/Dan3:19)

Shape \| verb pe'al, verb etpa''al

**Collocation:** זִיו שׁנה

to turn pale literally: (one's) complexion changes; hence: \= process by which one's complexion changes as a result of blood leaving the face

[DAN 5:6](https://ref.ly/Dan5:6) · [DAN 5:9](https://ref.ly/Dan5:9) · [DAN 5:10](https://ref.ly/Dan5:10) · [DAN 7:28](https://ref.ly/Dan7:28)

Shape \| verb pi, verb pu'al

**Collocation:** שׁנה פָּנֶה

to change (someone's) expression, to distort (someone's) face literally: to change (someone's) face; hence: \= causative process by which deities or circumstances cause the expression of someone's face to be changed

[JOB 14:20](https://ref.ly/Job14:20) · [ECC 8:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:1)

1 PRO 24:21 NIV "rebellious" NJPS "dissenters" REB "persons of high rank" GNT CEV "who rebel(s)" ESV "who do otherwise" \| RSV NRSV "either of them" \= שׁניהם 2 PSA 77:11 RSV NJPS NJB NRSV REB GNT CEV \| NIV NJPS "years of right hand" \= שָׁנָה

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Lamentations 4:1; Daniel 3:27; Daniel 7:3; Daniel 7:7; Daniel 7:19; Malachi 3:6; Esther 1:7; Proverbs 24:21; Esther 3:8; Daniel 2:9; Daniel 6:18; Daniel 7:23; Daniel 7:24; Ezra 6:11; Ezra 6:12; Psalms 89:35; Proverbs 31:5; Jeremiah 2:36; Daniel 2:21; Daniel 3:28; Daniel 4:13; Daniel 6:9; Daniel 6:16; Daniel 7:25; Psalms 77:11; 1 Samuel 21:14; Psalms 34:1; 2 Kings 25:29; Jeremiah 52:33; Genesis 41:32; 1 Samuel 26:8; 2 Samuel 20:10; 1 Kings 18:34; Nehemiah 13:21; Job 29:22; Proverbs 17:9; Proverbs 26:11; 1 Kings 14:2; Esther 2:9; Daniel 3:19; Daniel 5:6; Daniel 5:9; Daniel 5:10; Daniel 7:28; Job 14:20; Ecclesiastes 8:1


## שֶׁנְהַבִּים (id: 007531000000000)

שֶׁנְהַבִּים
============

שֶׁנְהַבִּים (H8143)

noun m

Substances

ivory \= a valued material; ◄ made from the tusk of elephants; ► used for construction and ornamentation

[1KI 10:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:22) · [2CH 9:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:21)

Sarah Lind

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 10:22; 2 Chronicles 9:21

שָׁנִי

## שְׁנִינָה (id: 007535000000000)

שְׁנִינָה
=========

שְׁנִינָה (H8148)

noun f · שׁנן

Despise \| ≈ חֶרְפָּה · מָשָׁל · קְלָלָה

taunt exact meaning unknown, probably: \= process when humans undergo ridicule from other humans, which is primarily expressed with words

[DEU 28:37](https://ref.ly/Deut28:37) · [1KI 9:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:7) · [2CH 7:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:20) · [JER 24:9](https://ref.ly/Jer24:9)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 28:37; 1 Kings 9:7; 2 Chronicles 7:20; Jeremiah 24:9


## שְׂנִיר (id: 007068000000000)

שְׂנִיר
=======

שְׂנִיר (H8149)

name

Names of Landforms \| ≈ חֶרְמֹון · שִׂיאֹון · שִׂרְיֹון

Senir \= mountain, also known as Hermon, Sion, and Sirion; ◄ part of the Anti\-Lebanon range; located north\-east of Israel

[DEU 3:9](https://ref.ly/Deut3:9) · [1CH 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:23) · [SNG 4:8](https://ref.ly/Song4:8) · [EZK 27:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:5)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 3:9; 1 Chronicles 5:23; Song of Songs 4:8; Ezekiel 27:5


## שׁנן (id: 007536000000000)

שׁנן
====

שׁנן (H8150)

verb · שֵׁן · שְׁנִינָה

Shape \| verb qal

to sharpen, to whet \= causative action by which humans use an iron tool to cause an object to have a thin cutting edge, ► in order to make it useful and effective as a tool; ≈ usually associated with aggression

[DEU 32:41](https://ref.ly/Deut32:41) · [PSA 45:6](https://ref.ly/Ps45:6) · [PSA 64:4](https://ref.ly/Ps64:4) · [PSA 120:4](https://ref.ly/Ps120:4) · [PSA 140:4](https://ref.ly/Ps140:4) · [PRO 25:18](https://ref.ly/Prov25:18) · [ISA 5:28](https://ref.ly/Isa5:28)

Impact \| verb hit

to be pierced, to be pricked literally: sharpened; hence: \= process by which part of the human body is pierced by a sharp object; ≈ used figuratively to denote an experience of anger and bitterness

[PSA 73:21](https://ref.ly/Ps73:21)

Know \| verb pi

to impress, to teach with diligence literally: to pierce (someone with knowledge); hence: \= causative action by which humans impart knowledge onto other humans with diligence

[DEU 6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:7)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 32:41; Psalms 45:6; Psalms 64:4; Psalms 120:4; Psalms 140:4; Proverbs 25:18; Isaiah 5:28; Psalms 73:21; Deuteronomy 6:7


## שׁנס (id: 007537000000000)

שׁנס
====

שׁנס (H8151)

verb

Dress \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁנס מָתְנַיִם

to gird one's loins, to tuck one's cloak into one's belt, to tie up one's skirts literally: to gird (one's) loins; hence: \= action by which humans pull up their robes and tie a belt or piece of cloth around their waist to keep it in place, ► in order to be able walk or work without being hampered

[1KI 18:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:46)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 18:46


## שִׁנְעָר (id: 007538000000000)

שִׁנְעָר
========

שִׁנְעָר (H8152)

name

Names of Locations

Shinar \= region; ◄ in Mesopotamia

[GEN 10:10](https://ref.ly/Gen10:10) · [GEN 11:2](https://ref.ly/Gen11:2) · [GEN 14:1](https://ref.ly/Gen14:1) · [GEN 14:9](https://ref.ly/Gen14:9) · [JOS 7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21) · [ISA 11:11](https://ref.ly/Isa11:11) · [DAN 1:2](https://ref.ly/Dan1:2) · [ZEC 5:11](https://ref.ly/Zech5:11)

Reinier de Blois

## שׁסה (id: 007539000000000)

שׁסה
====

שׁסה (H8154) — שׁשׂה

verb · שׁאס · שׁסס

Willing \| verb qal

## שֵׂעִיר (id: 007069000000000)

שֵׂעִיר
=======

שֵׂעִיר (H8165)

name · שָׂעִיר · שְׂעִירָה · שֵׂעָר · שַׂעֲרָה · שְׂעֹרָה

Names of People

Seir \= man; ► regarded as founder of an ethnic group bearing the same name, and living in an area named after him as well

[GEN 36:20](https://ref.ly/Gen36:20) · [GEN 36:21](https://ref.ly/Gen36:21) · [1CH 1:38](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:38)

Names of Groups

Seir \= Horite clan, named after the man who founded them and bearing the same name as the area they live in; they were later dispossessed by the Edomites and it is not always clear from the context whether it refers to the original Horites or the Edomites

[NUM 24:18](https://ref.ly/Num24:18) · [2CH 25:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:11) · [2CH 25:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:14) · [EZK 25:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:8)

Names of Locations

Seir \= mountainous area east of the Jordan valley and south of Moab, first inhabited by the Horites and later by the Edomites

to bring joy, to delight, to cheer poetic term; \= process by which divine intervention bring joy to humans

Rejoice \| verb pu'al

**Collocation:** שׁעע עַל־בִּרְכַּיִם

to dandle, to bounce poetic term; literally: to delight (someone) on (one's) knees; hence, probably: \= causative action by which humans bring joy to small children by bouncing them on their knees

[ISA 66:12](https://ref.ly/Isa66:12)

Towns

town literally: gate(s); hence: \= relatively large and permanent population center, surrounded by a wall and to be entered through a gate

## שׂער (id: 007073000000000)

שׂער
====

שׂער (H8175)

verb · שַׂעַר · שְׂעָרָה

Afraid \| verb qal

to be in terror \= process by which humans experience extreme fear

[JER 2:12](https://ref.ly/Jer2:12)

Afraid \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂער שַׂעַר

to be in terror \= process by which humans experience extreme fear

[EZK 27:35](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:35) · [EZK 32:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:10)

Afraid \| verb qal

to dread, to fear \= state in which humans stand in deep awe of a deity

[DEU 32:17](https://ref.ly/Deut32:17)

verb

Blow \| verb nif

storm \= process involving a strong movement of air, ► causing destruction; ≈ associated with the appearance and intervention of God

[PSA 50:3](https://ref.ly/Ps50:3)

Move \| verb pi

to sweep away literally: to storm (someone away); hence: \= causative action by which a strong wind causes humans to move their place, ≈ probably not to be understood literally but symbolic denoting people's downfall and death

[JOB 27:21](https://ref.ly/Job27:21)

Move \| verb qal

to sweep away literally: to storm (someone away); hence: \= causative process by which deities causes humans to move from their place with great force ◄ as if by a heavy storm; ≈ probably not to be understood literally but symbolic denoting people's downfall and death

[PSA 58:10](https://ref.ly/Ps58:10)

Move \| verb hit

to storm out literally: to storm (out against someone); hence: \= action by which a group of people moves forward quickly and aggressively ◄ like a strong wind

[DAN 11:40](https://ref.ly/Dan11:40)

Reinier de Blois

Crops

## שָׂפָה (id: 007081000000000)

שָׂפָה
======

שָׂפָה (H8193)

noun f · שָׂפָם

People

lip \= either of the two fleshy ridges forming the opening of the mouth, ► playing an important role in the speech and communication processes; ≈ sometimes used metonymically to denote the entire mouth or the activities performed with the lips, such as speech and language

[1SA 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:13) · [PRO 24:26](https://ref.ly/Prov24:26) · [SNG 4:3](https://ref.ly/Song4:3) · [SNG 4:11](https://ref.ly/Song4:11) · [SNG 5:13](https://ref.ly/Song5:13) · [SNG 7:10](https://ref.ly/Song7:10) · [ISA 6:7](https://ref.ly/Isa6:7) · [ISA 11:4](https://ref.ly/Isa11:4) · [DAN 10:16](https://ref.ly/Dan10:16) · [HAB 3:16](https://ref.ly/Hab3:16)

mouth literally: lips, used metonymically to denote the entire mouth; hence: \= the opening in the lower part of the face of animals where one places the bit in order to control them

[2KI 19:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:28) · [ISA 37:29](https://ref.ly/Isa37:29)

Stance \| verb hif

**Collocation:** פטר בְּשָׁפָה

to curl (one's) lips (to mock) exact meaning unknown; literally: to open with the lip; hence: \= action by which humans are curling their lips ► as a mocking gesture

[PSA 22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:8)

Stance \| verb qal

**Collocation:** קרץ שָׂפָה

to purse one's lips \= causative action by which humans press their lips together, ≈ which is associated with evil intent

[PRO 16:30](https://ref.ly/Prov16:30)

Speak

speech, words, talk, utterance lips, as a metonym for the speech that the lips help to form; hence: \= words that people speak

name · שׁפט

Shaphat \= man; ◄ son of Hori; tribe of Simeon; ► one of the twelve men sent by Moses to spy out the land

Shaphat \= man; ◄ from Abel\-Mehola; ► father of prophet Elisha

Shaphat \= man; ◄ tribe of Gad

[1CH 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:12)

Judge \| verb qal

שְׁפַטְיָה (H8203a)

name · שׁפט · יהוה

Shephatiah \= man; ◄ son of David

Names of People

Shephatiah \= man; ◄ tribe of Benjamin; ► father of Meshullam

[1CH 9:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:8)

Names of People

Shephatiah \= man; ► family head; after exile

[EZR 2:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:4) · [NEH 7:9](https://ref.ly/Neh7:9)

Names of People

Shephatiah \= man; ► family head; Solomon's servants; after exile

[EZR 2:57](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:57) · [EZR 8:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:8) · [NEH 7:59](https://ref.ly/Neh7:59)

Shephatiah \= man; ◄ tribe of Judah

[NEH 11:4](https://ref.ly/Neh11:4)

Names of People

Shephatiah \= man; ◄ son of Mattan; ► official; time of king Zedekiah

[JER 38:1](https://ref.ly/Jer38:1)

## שׁפך (id: 007581000000000)

שׁפך
====

שׁפך (H8210)

verb · שֶׁפֶךְ · שָׁפְכָה

Non\-Contain \| verb nif

to be poured out, to be spilled, to gush out \= process by which liquids or dry substances pour from a container when it is broken and unable to contain it anymore

[1KI 13:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:3) · [1KI 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:5) · [1KI 18:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:28) · [ZEP 1:17](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:17)

Non\-Contain \| verb qal, verb nif

to pour out \= causative action by which humans pour out a liquid or dry substance

[EXO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Exod4:9) · [EXO 29:12](https://ref.ly/Exod29:12) · [LEV 4:7](https://ref.ly/Lev4:7) · [LEV 4:18](https://ref.ly/Lev4:18) · [LEV 4:25](https://ref.ly/Lev4:25) · [LEV 4:30](https://ref.ly/Lev4:30) · [LEV 4:34](https://ref.ly/Lev4:34) · [LEV 14:41](https://ref.ly/Lev14:41) · [LEV 17:13](https://ref.ly/Lev17:13) · [DEU 12:16](https://ref.ly/Deut12:16) · [DEU 12:24](https://ref.ly/Deut12:24) · [DEU 12:27](https://ref.ly/Deut12:27) · [DEU 15:23](https://ref.ly/Deut15:23) · [JDG 6:20](https://ref.ly/Judg6:20) · [1SA 7:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:6) · [ISA 57:6](https://ref.ly/Isa57:6) · [AMO 5:8](https://ref.ly/Amos5:8) · [AMO 9:6](https://ref.ly/Amos9:6)

Non\-Contain \| verb qal

to cause to pour out, to spill \= causative process by which humans slash an opponent's body with a sword, after which the inner fluids and organs pour out of the victim's body like water

[2SA 20:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:10) · [JOB 16:13](https://ref.ly/Job16:13)

Chastise \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁפך זַעַם · שׁפך חֵמָה · שׁכף חֲרוֹן אַף · שׁפך עֶבְרָה

to pour out (one's) anger literally: to pour our (one's) anger; hence: \= action by which deities give full vent to their anger by punishing the offender

[PSA 69:25](https://ref.ly/Ps69:25) · [PSA 79:6](https://ref.ly/Ps79:6) · [ISA 42:25](https://ref.ly/Isa42:25) · [JER 6:11](https://ref.ly/Jer6:11) · [JER 10:25](https://ref.ly/Jer10:25) · [LAM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Lam2:4) · [LAM 4:11](https://ref.ly/Lam4:11) · [EZK 7:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:8) · [EZK 9:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:8) · [EZK 14:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:19) · [EZK 20:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:8) · [EZK 20:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:13) · [EZK 20:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:21) · [EZK 20:33](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:33) · [EZK 20:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:34) · [EZK 21:36](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:36) · [EZK 22:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:22) · [EZK 22:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:31) · [EZK 30:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:15) · [EZK 36:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:18) · [HOS 5:10](https://ref.ly/Hos5:10) · [ZEP 3:8](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:8)

Chastise \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁפך רָעָה

to requite (people) according to (their) evil actions literally: to pour out (someone's) evil (upon them); hence: \= action by which deities punish humans according to their actions

[JER 14:16](https://ref.ly/Jer14:16)

Complain \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁפך שִׂיחַ

to vent (one's) complaints literally: to pour our (one's) complaint (before someone else); hence: \= action by which humans give full vent to their complaints while praying to God

[PSA 102:1](https://ref.ly/Ps102:1) · [PSA 142:3](https://ref.ly/Ps142:3)

Dead \| verb hit

**Collocation:** שׁפך נֶפֶשׁ

to expire literally: (one's) life is poured out; hence: \= process by which humans slowly stop breathing and die

[JOB 30:16](https://ref.ly/Job30:16) · [LAM 2:12](https://ref.ly/Lam2:12)

Desire \| verb qal, verb nif

**Collocation:** שׁפך נְחֹשֶׁת · שׁפך תַּזְנוּת

to pour out (one's) lust, to lavish one's favors literally: to pour out (one's) lust; hence: \= action by which humans give full expression to their sexual desire

[EZK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:15) · [EZK 16:36](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:36) · [EZK 23:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:8)

Engage \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׂפך רוּחַ

to pour out (one's) spirit literally: to pour out (one's) spirit; hence: \= causative action by which deities impart their spirit on others

[EZK 39:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:29) · [JOL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Joel3:1) · [JOL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Joel3:2) · [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10)

Exist \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁפך סֹלֵלָה

to cast up a siege ramp \= causative action by which humans cast up an earthen mound against the wall of a city ► in order to enable to enemy to scale the wall

[2SA 20:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:15) · [2KI 19:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:32) · [ISA 37:33](https://ref.ly/Isa37:33) · [JER 6:6](https://ref.ly/Jer6:6) · [EZK 4:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:2) · [EZK 17:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:17) · [EZK 21:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:27) · [EZK 26:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:8) · [DAN 11:15](https://ref.ly/Dan11:15)

Fail \| verb pu'al

**Collocation:** אֲשֻׁרִים שׁפך

(one's) feet slip, to fail literally: (one's) steps are poured out; hence: \= process by which humans do not live up to the expectations others have of them

[PSA 73:2](https://ref.ly/Ps73:2)

Grieve \| verb nif

**Collocation:** כָּבֵד שׁפך לָאָרֶץ

to be deeply sad exact meaning unsure; literally: (one's) liver is poured out to the ground; hence, probably: \= state in which humans experience extreme grief

[LAM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Lam2:11)

Impact \| verb qal, verb nif

**Collocation:** שׁפך דָּם

to shed blood literally: to pour out blood; hence: \= causative action by which humans wound or kill (other) humans or animals

Know \| verb qal

**Collocation:** שׁפך לֵב · שׁפך לֵבָב · שׁפך נֶפֶשׁ

to pour out (one's) heart, to pour out (one's) soul literally: to pour out (one's) inner person (before someone else); hence: \= to communicate one's thoughts and emotions to someone else

[1SA 1:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:15) · [PSA 42:5](https://ref.ly/Ps42:5) · [PSA 62:9](https://ref.ly/Ps62:9) · [LAM 2:19](https://ref.ly/Lam2:19)

Scatter \| verb hit

to lie scattered literally: to be poured out; hence: \= state in which a number of objects lie in a haphazard way in a location, ◄ as if they were poured out from a large container

Shame \| verb qal

to pour contempt (on someone), to disgrace (someone) literally: to pour out contempt; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to experience total disgrace

[JOB 12:21](https://ref.ly/Job12:21) · [PSA 107:40](https://ref.ly/Ps107:40)

Weak \| verb nif

to be poured out \> to lose one's strength literally: to be poured out; hence: \= process by which humans gradually lose their physical strength ◄ as water is poured out of a container

[PSA 22:15](https://ref.ly/Ps22:15)

## שׁפל (id: 007584000000000)

שׁפל
====

שׁפל (H8213a · H8213b · A8214)

verb · שָׁפָל · שָׁפֵל · שֵׁפֶל · שִׁפְלָה · שְׁפֵלָה · שִׁפְלוּת

Low \| verb qal

to become low \= process by which an elevated place loses its elevation

[ISA 40:4](https://ref.ly/Isa40:4)

Low \| verb qal

to be low \= process by which an event takes place at a relatively low level of elevation

[ISA 29:4](https://ref.ly/Isa29:4)

Low \| verb hif

to do (something) low \= causative process by which humans or deities cause an event to take place at a relatively low level of elevation

[PSA 113:6](https://ref.ly/Ps113:6) · [ISA 57:9](https://ref.ly/Isa57:9)1

End \| verb qal \| ≈ שׁחח \| ≠ גֵּאֶה · גַּבְהוּת · רוּם

to be brought down, to be humbled literally: to be brought low; hence: \= process by which human pride is brought to an end, which often becomes visible in their attitude and appearance

[PRO 16:19](https://ref.ly/Prov16:19) · [ISA 2:11](https://ref.ly/Isa2:11) · [ISA 2:17](https://ref.ly/Isa2:17) · [ISA 5:15](https://ref.ly/Isa5:15)

End \| verb hif \| ≈ שׁבת

to bring down, to humble literally: to bring low; hence: \= causative process by which deities bring human pride to an end, which often becomes visible in their attitude and appearance

[PSA 18:28](https://ref.ly/Ps18:28) · [ISA 13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:11) · [ISA 25:11](https://ref.ly/Isa25:11)

Impact \| verb qal

to be brought down literally: to be brought low; hence: \= process by which high and imposing objects become low ◄ by falling or collapsing; ≈ associated with a loss of status

[ISA 10:33](https://ref.ly/Isa10:33) · [ISA 32:19](https://ref.ly/Isa32:19)

Impact \| verb hif \| ≈ שׁחח

to bring down literally: to bring low; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause high and imposing objects to become low ◄ by making them fall or collapse; ≈ associated with a loss of status

[ISA 25:12](https://ref.ly/Isa25:12) · [ISA 26:5](https://ref.ly/Isa26:5) · [ISA 26:5](https://ref.ly/Isa26:5) · [EZK 17:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:24)

Insignificant \| verb qal \| ≈ שׁחח

to be humbled, to be humiliated literally: to be brought low; hence: \= process by which humans lose their high status within a community

[ISA 2:9](https://ref.ly/Isa2:9) · [ISA 2:12](https://ref.ly/Isa2:12)2 · [ISA 5:15](https://ref.ly/Isa5:15)

Insignificant \| verb hif, verb haf'el \| ≠ גבהּ · עוד · רום

to humble, to humiliate literally: to bring low; hence: \= causative process by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to lose their high status within a community

[1SA 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:7) · [2SA 22:28](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:28) · [JOB 22:29](https://ref.ly/Job22:29) · [JOB 40:11](https://ref.ly/Job40:11) · [PSA 75:8](https://ref.ly/Ps75:8) · [PSA 147:6](https://ref.ly/Ps147:6) · [PRO 25:7](https://ref.ly/Prov25:7) · [PRO 29:23](https://ref.ly/Prov29:23) · [EZK 21:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:31) · [DAN 4:34](https://ref.ly/Dan4:34) · [DAN 5:19](https://ref.ly/Dan5:19) · [DAN 7:24](https://ref.ly/Dan7:24)

Insignificant \| verb hif imp

**Collocation:** הַשְׁפִּילוּ שֵׁבוּ

to step down, to take a lower position literally: bring low sit; hence: \= action by which humans give up their status and their privileges, ◄ described as coming from one's high throne and taking a seat at a lower level

[JER 13:18](https://ref.ly/Jer13:18)

Insignificant \| verb haf'el

**Collocation:** שׁפל לְבַב

to humble oneself literally: to bring low (one's) heart; hence: \= action by which humans think less highly of themselves than before

[DAN 5:22](https://ref.ly/Dan5:22)

Lax \| ≈ שׁחח

to become low, to fade away literally: to become low; hence: \= process by which an event is reduced in intensity

[ECC 12:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:4)

1 ISA 57:9 The underlying text of this clause may have been ותשׁפילי לשׁלח, with לשׁלח elided because the verb was mentioned in the previous clause. 2 ISA 2:12 MT LXX "ταπεινωθήσονται" NIV "be humbled" NJPS NJB ESV "brought low" \| RSV NRSV "high" REB "lifted up" GNT CEV "conceited"

Trees \| Fruits

almond tree, almond \= a tree belonging to the peach family; growing wild, and reaching a height of 4\.5 to 6 m; it sheds its oblong leaves in winter and produces white or pink flowers and blooms as early as January; the leaves appear much later; ◄ Prunis dulcis; ► the seed is fifty percent oil, and can be eaten raw or roasted

[GEN 21:23](https://ref.ly/Gen21:23) · [LEV 19:11](https://ref.ly/Lev19:11) · [1SA 15:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:29) · [PSA 44:18](https://ref.ly/Ps44:18) · [PSA 89:34](https://ref.ly/Ps89:34) · [ISA 63:8](https://ref.ly/Isa63:8)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 21:23; Leviticus 19:11; 1 Samuel 15:29; Psalms 44:18; Psalms 89:34; Isaiah 63:8


## שֹׁקֶת (id: 007629000000000)

שֹׁקֶת
======

שֹׁקֶת (H8268)

noun f · שׁקה

Containers \| ≈ רַהַט

watering trough \= receptacle, usually located near a well, where those who have drawn water can pour the water so that their animals can drink

[GEN 24:20](https://ref.ly/Gen24:20) · [GEN 30:38](https://ref.ly/Gen30:38)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 24:20; Genesis 30:38


## שֵׁר (id: 007630000000000)

שֵׁר
====

שֵׁר (H8285) — שֵׁרָה

noun f

Jewellery

bracelet \= object made by an artist; ◄ usually made out of precious metal; ► worn on the arm as decoration or charm

[ISA 3:19](https://ref.ly/Isa3:19)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 3:19

שַׂר
====

noun m · שׂרר

Officials \| Leaders \| ≈ אַדִּיר · זָקֵן · חֹר · יעץ · מֶלֶךְ · משׁל · נָגִיד · נָדִיב · רֹאשׁ · שׁטר · שׁפט

commander, chief, captain, dignitary, official, governor \= someone in a position of authority in an army, at a royal court, of a town, within a family, among priests, or elsewhere

## שׁרה (id: 007635000000000)

שׁרה
====

שׁרה (A8271 · H8281 · H8293) — שׁרא

verb · מִשְׁרָה · שִׁרְיָה

Free \| verb pe'al ptc.pass

loose, free \= state in which humans are untied and can walk around freely

[DAN 3:25](https://ref.ly/Dan3:25)

Safe \| verb pi

to deliver literally: to set free; hence: \= causative process by which deities deliver humans from difficult circumstances

[JER 15:11](https://ref.ly/Jer15:11)2

Begin

to begin literally: to release (an activity); hence: \= action by which humans start an activity

[EZR 5:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:2)

Happen \| verb qal

to unleash literally: to release (an event); hence: \= process by which deities cause an event to happen with force and without restraint

[JOB 37:3](https://ref.ly/Job37:3)

Know

to solve literally: to release (a problem); hence: \= action by which humans provide the answer to a difficult question

[DAN 5:12](https://ref.ly/Dan5:12) · [DAN 5:16](https://ref.ly/Dan5:16)

Weak

**Collocation:** קִטְרֵי חַרְצִין שׁרה

(one's) legs give way literally: the joints of one's hip are released; hence: \= process by which humans cannot stand up straight as a reaction to a strong emotion

[DAN 5:6](https://ref.ly/Dan5:6)

verb

Dwell \| verb pe'al

to dwell \= state in which a humans have made an area their home; ≈ only used figuratively to denote light having its home with God

[DAN 2:22](https://ref.ly/Dan2:22)

Reinier de Blois

## שִׁרְיֹון (id: 007643000000000)

שִׁרְיֹון
=========

שִׁרְיֹון (H8302b)

noun m · סִרְיֹון

Clothing \| Weapons \| ≈ בֶּגֶד · כֹּובַע · מָגֵן · מְעִיל · קֶלַע · קֶשֶׁת · רֹמַח · תִּלְבֹּשֶׁת

breastplate, chest protector \= a piece of armor covering the chest (and sometimes the back) from the neck to the waist; made of metal or thick leather reinforced with metal, to protect the body against blows and arrows

[1KI 22:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:34) · [2CH 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:33) · [2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14) · [NEH 4:10](https://ref.ly/Neh4:10) · [ISA 59:17](https://ref.ly/Isa59:17)

Clothing \| Weapons \| ≈ כֹּובַע · מַד · קֹובַע

**Collocation:** שִׁרְיֹון קַשְׂקַשִּׂים

coat of mail \= a kind of vest or shirt covering the upper part of the body and made of several hundred small bronze scales or platelets overlapping each other and sewn on a cloth or leather base; the scales were thin and about 11 by 6 centimeters; such armor was lighter than a solid metal breastplate and was also more flexible, giving greater maneuverability; used especially by soldiers who could not carry a shield but still needed protection in battle (for example, a chariot driver).

[1SA 17:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:5) · [1SA 17:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:5) · [1SA 17:38](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:38)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 22:34; 2 Chronicles 18:33; 2 Chronicles 26:14; Nehemiah 4:10; Isaiah 59:17; 1 Samuel 17:5; 1 Samuel 17:38

## שָׂרָף (id: 007117000000000)

שָׂרָף
======

שָׂרָף (H8314 · H8315)

noun m · שׂרף

Small Animals \| ≈ אֶפְעֶה · עַקְרָב · צֶפַע

carpet viper \= reddish brown viper with a pale underside and a row of pale blotches arranged side\-by\-side in pairs all the way down its back; it grows to about 80 centimeters in length, and its head is smaller and its body thinner than most typical vipers; it is easily provoked and quite aggressive; it is active mainly in the daytime, as it can stand higher temperatures than many other snakes; it lives in desert and grassland areas and feeds on jerboas, mice, and mole rats; its venom is slow\-acting but highly poisonous; ◄ \|iEchis coloratus\|i\*; ► unclean animal; ≈ associated with lurking danger

[NUM 21:6](https://ref.ly/Num21:6) · [NUM 21:8](https://ref.ly/Num21:8) · [DEU 8:15](https://ref.ly/Deut8:15) · [ISA 14:29](https://ref.ly/Isa14:29) · [ISA 30:6](https://ref.ly/Isa30:6)

seraph \= heavenly creature with six wings; ► surrounding the throne of God and praising his holiness

name · שׂרף

=======

noun m · שׁרשׁ

Vegetation \| ≈ גִּזַע · יֹונֵק · עָנָף · קָצִיר

Happen

root \> cause literally: root of a matter; hence: \= a person or event that gives rise to (another) event

root, base, sole literally: root; hence: \= a referent pointing to the bottom part of an object

## שׁרשׁ (id: 007652000000000)

שׁרשׁ
=====

שׁרשׁ (H8327)

verb · שֹׁרֶשׁ

Stage \| verb hif

**Collocation:** שׁרשׁ שֹׁרֶשׁ

to take root literally: to (take) root; hence: \= process by which a plant develops roots and so strengthens its position in the soil; ≈ associated with strength and permanence

[PSA 80:10](https://ref.ly/Ps80:10)

Stage \| verb hif, verb po'el, verb po'al

to take root literally: to root roots; hence: \= process by which a plant develops roots and so strengthens its position in the soil; ≈ associated with strength and permanence

[JOB 5:3](https://ref.ly/Job5:3) · [ISA 27:6](https://ref.ly/Isa27:6) · [ISA 40:24](https://ref.ly/Isa40:24) · [JER 12:2](https://ref.ly/Jer12:2)

Detach \| verb pu'al

to uproot literally: to root; hence: \= causative action by which humans pull out a plant from the soil together with its roots

[JOB 31:8](https://ref.ly/Job31:8)

Dead \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁרשׁ מֵאֶרֶץ חַיִּים

to uproot from the land of the living literally: to uproot from the land of the living; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to die an untimely death

[PSA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Ps52:7)

Impact \| verb pi

to consume, te devour \= causative process by which objects are completely destroyed

[JOB 31:12](https://ref.ly/Job31:12)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 80:10; Job 5:3; Isaiah 27:6; Isaiah 40:24; Jeremiah 12:2; Job 31:8; Psalms 52:7; Job 31:12


## שְׁרשִׁי (id: 007656000000000)

שְׁרשִׁי
========

שְׁרשִׁי (A8332) — שְׁרֹשִׁי

noun f

Impact

corporal punishment probably a Persian loanword; \= punishment inflicted on the human body1

[EZR 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:26)1

1 EZR 7:26 RSV NIV NJB NRSV REB GNT CEV derive this word from שֹׁרֶשׁ "root" and render it as "banishment" 2 EZR 7:26 MT\-K שׁרשׁו \| MT\-Q שְׁרֹשִׁי

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Ezra 7:26

[EST 7:1](https://ref.ly/Esth7:1)
